English: What are the climate and soil conditions for growing mangoes in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī rīera rīrīkū na tīīri ūrīkū wagīrīire wa kūhanda mīembe?

English: Mango farming success will depend on you growing a type that is best fit for your farm’s altitude, soil and climate.
Kikuyu: Urīmi mwega wa maembe ūringanaga na gūthūura mūthemba ūrīa waagīrīire itūra rīanyu, tīīri wa mūgūnda waku na rīera.

English: Using Kenya’s agro-ecological zoning, there are two major mango categories;
Kikuyu: Tūkīrora matūra ma kūrīma ma būrūri wa Kenya, mīthemba ya maembe nī īrī:

English: coastal/lowland varieties, these are fit for growing in areas of 0-800 MASL.
Kikuyu: Mīthemba īrīa īhandagwo gīcūa-inī kan kūndū kūrīa kwaraganu, mita kuuma 0nginya 800 igūrū wa iria.

English: Examples are the Ngowe
Kikuyu: Kwa mūhiano Ngowe.

English: You will find their huge populations in Mombasa, Kilifi, Tana-River and Lamu areas.
Kikuyu: Nī ūkuona mīthemba īno kwa wūingī matūra-inī ma gīcūa-inī ta Mombasa, Kilifi, Tana-River na Lamu.

English: mid altitude varieties.
Kikuyu: Mīthemba ya matūra matarī irīma nene:

English: These do well in areas of 800-1600 metres above sea level.
Kikuyu: Mīthemba īno yagīraga matūra-inī marīa marī mita 800 nginya 1600 igūrū wa iria.

English: They are common in Embu, Meru, Muranga, Marakwet, Kitui, Machakos and Makueni.
Kikuyu: Mahandagwo mūno Embu, Merū, Mūrang’a, Marakwet, Kitui, Machakos na Makueni.

English: Which varieties of mango are for low and altitudes?
Kikuyu: Nī mīthemba īrīkū ya maembe yagīrīire kūndū kūrīa kwaraganū?

English: Varieties for lowland (0-700m a.s.l) are Dodo, Ngowe, Boribo and Apple.
Kikuyu: Mīthemba ya kūndū kūrīa kwaraganu (0 – 700m.a.s.l.) nī Dodo, Ngowe, Boribo, na Apple.

English: Varieties for medium altitude areas (1500m a.s.l) are Tommy Atkins, Kent, Haden, van Dyke Sensation and Alphonso
Kikuyu: Mīthemba ya kūndū gūtarī īrīma na heho nyingī(Medium Altitude Areas) (1500 m.a.s.l) nī Tommy Atkins, Kent, Haden van Dyke, Sensation na Alphonso.

English: What are the ecological requirements for growing mangoes in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū mabatarainie ūrīmi-inī wa maembe?

English: Mangoes in Kenya grow well at an altitude below 1000 mm.
Kikuyu: Maembe makūraga wega matūūra-inī marīa mari thī wa mita 1000 igūrū rīa iria būrūri-inī wa Kenya .

English: However, other cultivars are reported to yield well at an altitude of up to 1800 mm.
Kikuyu: Ona kūrīo ūguo, nī kūrī mīthemba īkaga wega matūūra-inī marī igūrū wa mita 1800 igūrū rīa iria.

English: Mango is known to yield well in a warm tropical monsoon climate.
Kikuyu: Maembe moīkaine kūheana magetha mega kūndū kūrīa kūrī na ūrugarī.

English: It needs a prolonged dry period of about three months, followed by rain.
Kikuyu: Mendaga ihinda rīa mīeri ītatū ya riūa, gūgīcoka kuura.

English: In regions that do not vary in rainfall or temperature, your trees will not produce any fruits.
Kikuyu: Kūria mbura na ūrugarī itacenjacenjagia, mīembe ndīciaraga.

English: Temperature range – 15º C to 30º C
Kikuyu: 1.Ūrugarī ūceragīra 15⁰ C nginya 30⁰C.

English: 2. Rainfall - Annual 500mm-1500mm
Kikuyu: 2.Mīembe yendaga mbura mūigana wa milimita 500 nginya 1500 o mwaka.

English: 3. Soils - Deep (3m) fertile, well drained and optimum pH 5.5-7.5
Kikuyu: 3. tīīri - Mūriku (mita ithatū), mūnoru na ūrūrū ūtarakīra gīthimo gīa pH 5.5 nginya 7.5

English: 4. Altitude- 0-1500m above sea level
Kikuyu: 4. matūūra - kuuma 0 nginya mita 1500  igūrū ria iria

English: What are the best growing conditions for mango?
Kikuyu: Hihi nī maūndū marīkū magīrīire ūkūria-inī wa maembe?

English: Mango trees need a warm environment with a temperature ideally above 21°C (70°F) and a minimum temperature of 4°C (40°F).
Kikuyu: Mīembe yendaga ūrugarī ūkīrīte  21⁰ C(70⁰ F) na ūtanyihīire 4⁰ C (40⁰F).

English: Mango trees ideally need 8 hours of sunlight per day to produce flowers and fruit.
Kikuyu: Maembe mendaga ihinda rīa mathaa manana o mūthenya ma riūa nīguo mahote kūruta kīro na matunda.

English: Place pot-grown mango trees outside in summer if possible, to give them sufficient sunlight
Kikuyu: Mīembe īkūrīirio nyūngū-inī thīinī wa nyūmba nī yendaga kuumio nja yote riūa.

English: When is soil testing for mangoes recommended?
Kikuyu: tīīri wa mūgūndwa wa mīembe wagīrīirwo gūthimwo rī?

English: Soil testing is recommended before planting to guide on fertilizer and manure application
Kikuyu: Wagīrīire gūthimwo mīembe ītanahandwo nīgūo gūteithia kūmenya bataraitha na thumu ūrīa wagīrīire kūhūthīrwo.

English: Which fertilizer are used to induce flowering and promote good fruit set, size and continued growth at flowering and fruiting stage?
Kikuyu: Nī bataraitha īrīkū īteithagia mūembe kūruta kīro, gwīkira matunda na kūmatunguhia?

English: Foliar fertilizer with boron and potassium
Kikuyu: Nī bataraitha ya mathangū yongereirwo boron na potassium

English: Best time to apply fertilizers in mango?
Kikuyu: Nī ihinda rīrīkū rīega rīa gwikīra bataraitha mīembe-inī?

English: After about 3-6 months, start application of nitrogenous fertilizers (in case of fruit trees e.g. mango, pawpaw, oranges).
Kikuyu: Īkīra bataraitha mīeri ītatū nginya ītandatū thutha wa kūhanda mītī ya matunda ta mīembe, mababaī na macungwa.

English: Apply nitrogenous fertilizers in varied intervals to avoid leaching out (downward movement of useful mineral or nutrients in water).
Kikuyu: īkīra bataraitha ya nitrogen kwa ithimi nini ūtigithukanītie nīguo ndīkore nathī kana ona na maaī.

English:
Alternatively, properly prepared farm yard manure or compost, kitchen ash (left from cooking) and mulching can be
used in place of fertilizers.
Kikuyu: Ona ningī mītī no īkīro thumu mūhīu wega, mūhu na kwarīra mūceng'i handū ha bataraitha.

English: Trees should be top-dressed with 250g of CAN per tree at the beginning of each rainy season after the trees attain a height of 1 m.
Kikuyu: Nīwega kūhe mūtī wothe ūrī na ūraihu wa makīria ma mita īmwe bataraitha ya kūrūmīrīria mūthemba wa CAN mūigana wa gramu magana merī ma mīrongo ītano.

English: What is the soil mixture for mangoes?
Kikuyu: Tīīri wa mīembe ūtukanagio atīa?

English: 50% native soil: Your mango's local comfort food.
Kikuyu: 50% - tīīri; Irio cia kīūndūire cia mīembe.

English: 25% organic compost: The secret sauce for fertility.
Kikuyu: 25% thumu wa gūthondekwo na mahuti: Hitho ya ūnoru.

English: 25% sand: For that just-right drainage
Kikuyu: 25% mūthanga: wa kūrigīrīria kwaraha kwa maaī ma mbura

English: What is the best NPK fertilizer for mango trees?
Kikuyu: Nī bataraitha īrīkū ya NPK yagīrīire mīembe?

English: Mango trees have a thing for potassium.
Kikuyu: Mīembe īkenagio mūno nī potassium.

English: It's like their secret sauce for a bountiful harvest.
Kikuyu: Nīyo hitho yayo ya kūingīhia maciaro.

English: An 8-3-9 fertilizer hits the sweet spot, offering a potassium-rich mix that doesn't go overboard on the nitrogen.
Kikuyu: Mūtukanio wa bataraitha wa 8-3-9 ūkoragwo ūrī mwega tondu ndūkīragia Nitrogen na nīūheaga mūembe potassium ya kūigana.

English: Remember, more leaves don't mean more fruit
Kikuyu: Nīwega kūmenya atī wūingī wa mahuti tikuo kūingīhia matunda.

English: When should I put my mango seed in soil?
Kikuyu: Hihi nīrī batiī kūhanda mbeū ya mūembe tīīri-inī?

English: Mango seeds are best planted in the summer, as there are many hours of sunlight, helping the tree grow.
Kikuyu: Mbeū cia mīembe ciendaga kūhandwo hīndī īrīa kūri na riūa inene,  nīgetha īkagia na mahinda manene ma rīua, rīrīa rītūmaga mūtī ukure na ihenya.

English: Is cow manure good for mango trees?
Kikuyu: Hihi thumu wa ng’ombe nī mwega kūrī mīembe?

English: Cow dung mixed with water should be used as fertilizer and mixed into soil near the base of a newly planted mango tree.
Kikuyu: Rūrūa rūngītukanio na maaī nīrūhūthīkaga ta bataraitha na rūtukanagio na tīīri gītīna-inī kia mūembe.

English: Manure is a great source of nutrients and can be treated very easily with the help of some simple methods
Kikuyu: Thumu nī kīhumo kiega mūno kia irio cia mūembe na no ūharīrio wega na njīra hūthū cigana ūna.

English: Is chicken manure good for mango trees?
Kikuyu: Hihi thumu wa ngūkū nī mwega kūrī mīembe?

English: Do NOT use chicken manure or fertilise too soon after planting.
Kikuyu: Ndūgekīre thumu wa ngūkū kana bataraitha na ihenya thutha wa kūhanda mīembe yaku.

English: DO water regularly (every second day).
Kikuyu: Itagīrīria mīembe yaku maaī o thutha wa thikū igīrī.

English: What is the best way to prune mangoes?
Kikuyu: Mīembe īcehagwo atīa?

English: The best way to keep a mango tree small is to prune it when it is young.
Kikuyu: Mūembe wagīrīirwo gūcehwo ūrīmū nini.

English: As a general rule, prune any branch that is more than a meter in length to about a meter.
Kikuyu: Kaingī nī wega gūceeha honge ciothe iria ikīrītie ūraihu wa mita īmwe ūcitige irī cia mita īmwe.

English: Then make additional cuts every time the branches grow to eight inches.
Kikuyu: Rūmīrīra gūceha honge ciakinyia ūraihu wa inji inyanya.

English: After every harvest you should make another pruning to prepare the tree for the next season and to keep it healthy.
Kikuyu: O maembe magethwo, mītī īgacehwo īkīharīrīrio kīmera kīria kīrumīrīire na kūmīiga īrī na hinya.

English: This should be done with lopping shears, you can also prune dead branches and those with diseases.
Kikuyu: Ceeha ūkīhūthīra magathī ūkīrutaga honge īria nguū na iria ndwaru.

English: Make sure the soil is moist but not drenched.
Kikuyu: Tigagīrīra tīīri nī mūigū no ti mūcagacu.

English: Let the soil dry out almost completely between watering.
Kikuyu: Rekaga tīīri ūūme hakuhībiū ūtanahe maaīringī.

English: Water your mango tree regularly, but do not overwater it.
Kikuyu: Heyaga mīembe maaī maita maingī, no ndūgakīragie.

English: What are the challenges experienced by mango farmers in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī mathīna marīkū arīmi a maembe būrūri-inī wa Kenya matūnganaga namo?

English: Pest and diseases – Mango pests and diseases can wipe out your mango orchard, or make your mango harvest be of inferior quality, leading to losses.
Kikuyu: Mīrimū na tūtambi: Mīrimū na tūtambi no īgūthūkīrie mūgūnda wothe kana ītūme mīembe īciare matunda matarī mega na gūcūngīrīria hathara.

English: Poor Management – Most mango farmers ignore their mango trees, only paying attention during harvest time.
Kikuyu: Mūtungatīre mūru wa mīembe: Arīmī aingī nī magaga gūtungata mīembe igīkūra na kwīrūmbūiya tu hīndī ya magetha.

English: The trees are left to scatter on the farm and grow as they wish, without any activities like pruning, disease control, pest control, etc.
Kikuyu: Mītī ītigagwo īkūre o urīa īkwenda, itagūcehwo kana kūgitīrwo kuumana na mīrimū, tūtambi, na maūndū mangī.

English: Proper management activities of the mango orchard can lead to good quality harvests, hence more income.
Kikuyu: Mīembe īngītungatwo wega no īgīe na magetha mega na kūingīhia umithio.

English: Poor market identification – Most mango farmers, due to poor planning, are not able to identify a market for their produce early enough.
Kikuyu: Kwaga kūmenya thoko njega ya maembe. Nīūndū wa kwaga kwibanga wega, arīmī matikoragwo mooī kūrīa makendia maembe mao.

English: This leaves them at the mercy of brokers, who dictate meager prices.
Kikuyu: Ūndū ūyū ūtigaga arīmi moko-inī na brokers iria ithukagia thogora wa maembe.

English: If you plan early, project your harvest, and identify a target market, then mango farming will be a lucrative venture.
Kikuyu: Mūrīmi angībanga wega, amenye akagetha maembe maigana atīa, na amenye akendia kū, ūrīmi wa maembe no ūkorwo ūrī biashara ya kwīrirīrio.

English: Poor Planting Material – Most mango farmers germinate seeds from the available indigenous seeds, instead of going for the grafted seedlings that start bearing fruit earlier than the ungrafted counterparts.
Kikuyu: Mbegū ītakinyanīirie ikīro: Arīmi aingī mahandaga mbeū cia kīmerera handū ha īrīa jiarithanie iria iciaraga mbere ya īrīa ītarī jiarithanie.

English: Grafted mangoes are easier to manage and are more productive.
Kikuyu: Mīembe mīciarithanie nī mīhūthū gūtungata na īciaraga mūno.

English: Post-harvest Losses – Mangoes in Kenya are harvested during one season.
Kikuyu: Gūthūka kwa maembe magethwo: Maembe būrūri-inī wa Kenya makorawo na kīmera kīmwe.

English: This leads to a glut in the market.
Kikuyu: Ūndū ūyū ūtūmaga kūgie na wūingī wamo thoko-inī.

English: During the mango season, lots of fruit goes to waste because of a lack of proper storage and transport to the market.
Kikuyu: Hīndī ya magetha, maembe maingī nī moraga nīundū wa kwaga kūndū kwega gwa kūiga na miumīrīrie mīega.

English: When do you irrigate mango trees?
Kikuyu: Hihi mīembe īitagīrīrio maaī rī?

English: Mango trees need less water than other commercial fruit tree farming like banana farming.
Kikuyu: Mīembe ībataraga maaī manīnī gūkīra mīmera īrīa īngī ya matunda ta marigū.

English: Mango farm should be irrigated at an interval of two to three days, in the first year of your mango farming business.
Kikuyu: Mīembe yagīrīirwo gūitīīrīrio maaī o thutha wa mīthenya īrī kana ītatū mwaka-īnī wa mbere.

English: However, at the stage when they start to bear fruit about two irrigations are required.
Kikuyu: Ona kūrī ūguo, mīembe yambīrīria gūciara yendaga maaī maita meerī.

English: You must give water to the fruits bearing plant at an interval of 10 – 15 days.
Kikuyu: Mīembe iciarīte ībatiī gūitīīrīrīo maaī o thutha wa thikū ikūmi nginya ikūmi na ithano.

English: Irrigation should be stopped after their full growth.
Kikuyu: Mīembe ītigaga gūitīīrīrīo maaī yarīkia kūgimara.

English: Take proper care during the flowering stage.
Kikuyu: Mīembe ībatiī kūmenyererwo makīria rīrīa īkūīte kīro.

English: During this stage, stop giving them water.
Kikuyu: Hīndī īno,mīembe ndīkaheo maaī.

English: However, if irrigation does not stop, it may lead to bad quality mango fruit production.
Kikuyu: īngīaga gūtiga kūheo maaī, mīembe no īciare matunda matarī mega.

English: How to control weeds in mangoes?
Kikuyu: Mūgūnda wa mīembe ūrīmagīrwo atīa?

English: In order to manage weeds effectively in mango orchards farmers should practice chemical weed control.
Kikuyu: Nīguo kūnina riia biū mīgūnda-inī ya mīembe, arīmi magīrīirūo kūhūthīra dawa cia ria.

English: This method is cost effective and saves time.
Kikuyu: īno nī njīra itarī goro mūnene na īhonokagia mahinda.

English: Chemical weeding involves spraying Catapult® 480 SL 200 ml/20l to actively growing weeds.
Kikuyu: Dawa ya kūhuhīra ria nī ta Catapult® 480 SL īrīa ītukanagio milimita magana merī na rita mīrongo īrī cia maaī.

English: The spray should not come into contact with leaves or stems of young trees.
Kikuyu: Dawa ya ria ndībatīī kūhutīa mathangū ma mītī ya mīembe kana itina cia mītī īrīa mīnini.

English: When should pruning of mangoes start?
Kikuyu: Mītī ya mīembe ībatīī gūcehwo kuuma rī?

English: Pruning should start early for shaping the tree.
Kikuyu: Guceha kūbatīe kwambīrīria o tene nīgetha mītīīige wega.

English: At 1m high, cut the main shoot back to 0.6-0.7 m below the ring buds with secateurs.
Kikuyu: Mītī ya ūraihū wa mita īmwe, ītīnio kīongo na magathī ītīgwo īrī na ūraihu wa 0.6m nginya 0.7m.

English: After new growth select only 3-4 outward shoots to grow to branches to 1m length, then cut them back to 50-75 cm length again.
Kikuyu: Mutī wahethūka ūthuure makunū matatū kana mana na marekwo makure nginya makinyie ūraihu wa mita īmwe, macio namo matinio makuhīhe nginya sentimita 50 nginya 75.

English: Repeat the same process twice. Ideal mango tree should have 3-4 main trunks open and be 4-5 meters high.
Kikuyu: Cokera o ūguo maita mangī merī. Mūembe ūria mwega ubatiī gūkorwo na honge ithatū kana inya na ikorwo na ūraihu wa mita inya nginya ithano.

English: How should established mango trees be pruned?
Kikuyu: Mīembe mīgīma īcehagwo atīa?

English: Pruning established trees will involve removing all diseased or dead branches to keep infestation from spreading.
Kikuyu: Guceha mītī mīgima nī kūruta honge ciothe iria ndwaru na nyūmū nīgetha mīrimū ndīgathereme.

English: Remove hanging branches to keep fruit from dragging on the ground.
Kikuyu: Ceha honge ciothe icunjurīte nīguo matunda matikahutie tīīri.

English: Removal of vertical shoots as opposed to horizontal ones for tree shaping and maintaining the right height.
Kikuyu: Ceha honge īria irūngiī na ūtige iria ngīgamu īria iheaga mūtī mūhianīre mwega na ūraihu wa ūria wagīrīire.

English: Trim horizontal branches to 50cm length.
Kikuyu: Ceha honge ciothe ngīgamu ūtigie irī na ūraihu wa sentimita 50.

English: Open the tree center by removing cluttered branch to allow air and sunlight to reach all parts of the trees to stimulate growth and for ease of spray for any infections.
Kikuyu: Hingura mūtī na njīra ya kweheria honge iria ndumanu gatagatī nīgetha rīera rīcere, ūtheri wa riūa ūkinyīre mūtī wothe na kūhūthia mīhūrīre ya dawa.

English: Pruning should be done 2-4 weeks before flowering begins
Kikuyu: Mītī yagīrīirwo gūcehwo wiki-inī ya kerī nginya ya kana mbere ya īrute mahūa.

English: When is the best time to prune mangoes?
Kikuyu: Nī ihinda rīrīkū mīembe ibatiī gūcehwo?

English: Should be done after harvesting period is over (March-April) before the main flush in May.
Kikuyu: Thutha wa kūgetha maembe (March – April), no ningī mbere ya kīro gūīkīra mweri-inī wa gatano.

English: What is the procedure to prune mangoes?
Kikuyu: Mūtaratara ūria mwega wa gūceha mīembe nī ūrīkū?

English: - Open the tree centre by removing all the branches growing towards it
Kikuyu: Ceha gatagatīka mūembe ūkīrutaga honge ciothe iria irakūra irorete gitina.

English: - Remove all the branches crisscrossing and those which are shaded
Kikuyu: Ruta honge ciothe iria ihīngīcanīte na iria ihumbīranīte.

English: - Remove die bark and dried twigs
Kikuyu: Ruta honge ciothe iria ciumīte mīthia-inī na iria nyūmū.

English: - Remove branches growing downwards such that the lowest branch should be at least 1m above the ground
Kikuyu: Ruta honge ciothe irorete na thī nīguo atī rūhonge rūrīa rūgūkorwo rūkuhīrīirie tīīri rūraihīrīrie na mita īmwe.

English: One should be careful not to over open the centre-the open space should be about 1/4 of the canopy
Kikuyu: Menyerera ndūgacehe gītagatī kīa mūtī makīria, eheria rūhonge rūmwe o harī inya (1/4).

English: How to induce flowering in mangoes?
Kikuyu: Hihi mīembe īngīkwo atīa nīguo īrute kīro?

English: This can be done through:
Kikuyu: ūndū ūyū no wīkwo na njīra ya:

English: Cultural Methods
Kikuyu: Njīra cia kīndūire:

English: - Smudging (moist organic material – grass, leaves etc. is slowly burnt under the tree canopies and the resulting smoke induces flowering)
Kikuyu: Gūcinīra mahuti rungu rwa mūembe kahora nīguo mūtī ūtogererwo na wītīkīre kūruta mahūa.

English: - Ringing the tree branches
Kikuyu: Gūkurura honge cia mūembe ūthiūrūrūkītie.

English: - Pruning the roots
Kikuyu: Guceha mīri.

English: - Applying salts on the roots
Kikuyu: Kūhaka mīri cumbī.

English:
Chemical Methods
- Application of Potassium Nitrate (1%) Solution mixed with a sticker agent (adhesive) on the tree totally drenching its terminals and leaves
Kikuyu: Ūhūthīri wa dawa; - Kūhūra mītī wega mūtukanio wa dawa ya Potassium Nitrate (1%) na sticker na ūgītigagīrira nīyahutia irūngo na mathangū.

English: - If the timing is right, flowering will emerge 10-14 days after application
Kikuyu: Angīkorūo ihinda nīrīagīrīire, mahūa makoneka thikū 10 nginya 14 thutha wa kūhūra dawa īno.

English: - The readiness of the tree to flower is an important factor for a successful operation.
Kikuyu: Gūkorwo mūtī wīharīirie kūruta kīro nīūndū wa bata mūno.

English: Best results are achieved on trees with leaves that are dull green or greenish-brown and brittle when crushed by hand.
Kikuyu: Njīra īno yonanagia moimīrīra mega mītī-inī īna mathangū matarī maruru kana ona maragaruruka rangi magatwīka ma gītīri na kūnīkanga na inegene mahehenjwo na gūoko.

English: The tree should have an appearance of suspended growth or be dormant - Auster or Culter (Paclobutrazol)
Kikuyu: Ningī nī mūtī ūhana gūtindima kana ūtaraciara-Auster kana Culter (Paclobutrazol)

English: - New product in Kenya. Also regulate production and one gets much higher yields
Kikuyu: īno nī dawa njerū būrūri-inī wa Kenya. Na nīīteithagia gūtereba maciaro na ona gūtūma maingīhe.

English: Mangoes thrives well in which soils?
Kikuyu: Mīembe yagagīra tīīri-inī ūhana atīa?

English: Mango thrives well on a wide variety of soils, provided they are not too waterlogged, too alkaline or too shallow. od.
Kikuyu: Mīembe yagīraga wega matī īri-inī wa mīthemba mīingī, angīkorwo matigūcagaca, kūrūra gīthemba gia tīīrī wa iria-inī, kana ti mariku wega.

English: A pH of 5.5 – 7.5 is preferred. Deep well drained, loam or clay is go
Kikuyu: Ūrūrū wa tīīrī wa kuuma pH ya 5.5 – 7.5 nīguo wagīrīire. tīīri ūraikūrūkia maaī wega, mūthemba wa loam kana clay nīguo mwega.

English: When do a mango seedling start to bear fruits?
Kikuyu: Mūembe ūciaraga thutha wa ihinda rīigana atīa?

English: Mango seedlings start to bear fruit within 4-7 years, while grafted trees (if allowed) may bear a few fruits in their second year in the field
Kikuyu: Mīembe iciaraga thutha wa miaka īna ngīnya mūgwanja, no īrīa mīciarithanie (īngītīkīrio) no īciare kuuma mwaka-inī wa kerī.

English: Does intercropping mangoes with other crops reduce weeding?
Kikuyu: Hihi kūhanda indo ingī mīembe-inī nīkūgiragia mūgūnda ūgīe ria?

English: An intercrop in the first three years will reduce the cost of wedding.
Kikuyu: Kūhandīrīra indo ingī mīaka-inī ya mbere ītatū nīkūnyihagia ngarama ya kūrīmīra.

English: After the 4th year, the canopy will have reduced the weed incidences
Kikuyu: Thutha wa mwaka wa kana, kīīruru kia mīembe nīkīgiragia ria kūmera.

English: How do mangoes with potassium deficiency look like?
Kikuyu: Mūembe ūtaiganītie Potassium ūhanaga atīa?

English: -It is characterized by necrosis of leaf margin which starts from the tip downwards.
Kikuyu: Ūgīaga na mathangū marī na ironda īria ciambagīrīria mīthia-inī.

English: -Fruit quality is highly reduced.
Kikuyu: Matunda matikoragwo mega ona hanini.

English: -Disease and pest attack as secondary infections
Kikuyu: Mīrimū na tūtambi ikaugūmīra.

English: How do you control potassium deficiency in mangoes
Kikuyu: Hihi ūnyihu wa potassium ūrūagwo naguo atīa?

English: Manage by applying muriate of potash, urea and super phosphate fertilizer
Kikuyu: Rūa naguo na njīra ya gwīkīra bataraitha mīthemba ya muriate ya potash,urea na super phospate.

English: Boron deficiency symptoms?
Kikuyu: Ndariri cia ūnyihu wa Boron nī irīkū?

English: -It is characterized by flower abortion, and also cracking of fruits.
Kikuyu: Mūembe wūitaga kīro na matunda magakaragacuka.

English: -Brown areas can also be seen in yellow fruit pulp
Kikuyu: Maroro ma rangi wa tīīri marīa marī igūru wa iembe no maingīre nginya thīinī.

English: How to control boron deficiency
Kikuyu: Kīhonia kīa ūnyihu wa boron nī kīrīkū?

English: Apply 250gm boron per tree (10-15-year-old).
Kikuyu: He mītī yothe īna ūkūrū wa mīaka ikūmi nginya ikūmi na ītano mūigana wa gramu 250 ya boron o mūtī.

English: Copper deficiency symptoms in mangoes and how to manage?
Kikuyu: Ndariri cia wagi wa coppers mīembe-inī nīi rīkū na ingīrūo nacio atīa?

English: Common in young tree due to heavy nitrogenous fertilizer, the terminal shoot is weak, defoliation and die back of branches, drooping shaped branches. spray copper or copper oxychloride (0.3%) at monthly interval
Kikuyu: Ndariri ici cionekaga kaingī mīembe-inī mīnini irīa īhetwo bataraitha ya nitrogen īgakīra, mūrūri ndūrī hinya, kūgūithia mathangū, kūūma kwa mīthia ya honge, kūinama kwa honge. Hūūra Copper kana Copper Oxychloride (0.3%) o thutha wa mweri ūmwe.

English: How can I make my mango tree grow faster?
Kikuyu: Ingīka atīa nīguo mūembe wakwa ūkūre na ihenya makīria?

English: Plant the tree in a spot with full sunlight and well-draining soil.
Kikuyu: Handa mūembe handū harakinyīrwo nī riūa na tīīri-inī ūraikūrūkia maaī wega.

English: Fertilization: Use a balanced fertilizer with higher nitrogen content to encourage fast growth during the growing season.
Kikuyu: Hūthīra bataraitha thondeke wega īrī na nitrogen nyingī yagūteithia mūtī gūkūra naihenya.

English: Watering: Water regularly, especially during the first few years
Kikuyu: Mīhe maaī kaingī, makīria mīaka-inī ya kīambīrīria.

English: What are the best intercrops for mangoes?
Kikuyu: Nī mīmera īrīkū īngīhandwo mīembe-inī?

English: Many plants such as coconut, areca, lemon, guava, papaya, fig, banana, jack, pepper, can be grown as intercrops.
Kikuyu: Nī mīmera mīingī ta mīnathi, areca, ndimū, mbera, mababaī, mūgumo, marigū, biribiri, jack, biribiri no īhandanīrio.

English: Why is my mango not growing?
Kikuyu: Mīembe yakwa īraga gūkūra nīkī?

English: There could be several factors that are causing this, such as a lack of nutrients, water, or sunlight.
Kikuyu: Nīkūrī itūmi nyingī īngīgiria mīembe īkūre wega ta kwaga irio, maaī na riūa rīa kūigana.

English: Here are some steps you can take to help restore its growth:
Kikuyu: Maūndū maya no mateithie gūcokereria gūkūra kwa mūembe:

English: Check the soil: Ensure that your mango plant is planted in nutrient-rich soil that drains well
Kikuyu: Rora tīīri: Tigīrīra mūembe nī wahandwo tīīri-inī mūnoru na ūtaraiga maaī.

English: How do you increase pollination in mangoes?
Kikuyu: Njīra cia kuongerera gūciarana kwa mīembe nī irīkū?

English: The method comprises the following specific steps:
Kikuyu: Njīra īyo īkoragwo na makinya maya:

English: breeding flies one month before the flowing phase of the mango;
Kikuyu: Gūciarithania ngi mweri ūmwe mbere ya mīembe kūruta mahūa;

English: inducing flies with discarded livestock entrails or dead fish or rotten meat to pollinate the mango in the full-bloom phase; .
Kikuyu: Nyama kana thamaki īrabutha īngīteo kūu mūgūnda-inī nīīgūtūma ngi iciaranīre kuo,iria iteithagia gūciarithania kwa mītī rīrīa īracanūka biū.

English: and killing and removing flies in the mango garden after flowers falling
Kikuyu: na ngi icio ciūragwo kīro gīaītīka.

English: Why do mangoes split on tree?
Kikuyu: Nīkīī gītūmaga maembe matūke marī mītī-inī?

English: The unfavorable weather conditions (high temperature, heavy rainfall and humidity) may be the main reasons for fruit splitting in mango.
Kikuyu: Rīera rītagīrīire, ta ūrugarī mūingī, mbura na ūhehu mūnene, no itūme maembe maatūke.

English: The main reason why the mango trees flower but not setting fruit is anthracnose disease which attack all part of the trees but most damage occurs to the flower panicles
Kikuyu: Gītūmi kīrīa kīnene gītūmaga mīembe īrute kīro noo yage gūciara nī mūrimū mūrū wa anthracnose ūrīa ūhutagia handū hothe ha mītī īyo, no mūno ūthūkagia mahūa.

English: What causes premature fruit drop in mangoes?
Kikuyu: Maembe maitīkaga nīk īmatarī makūrū?

English: They include dry weather or too much rain, pests, diseases and lack of fertiliser.
Kikuyu: Maembe mangīitīka nīūndū wa ihinda inene rīa riūa, mbura nyīngī, tūtambi, mīrimū ona kwaga bataraitha.

English: Wrong time of fertilisation can cause the young fruits to fall off as well
Kikuyu: Bataraitha īngīheo mītī ihinda rīrīa rītagīrīire nī ītūmaga matunda marīa manini magwe.

English: What is Propagation?
Kikuyu: Hihi propagation nīkīī?

English: It is the process by which new plants grow from various sources: seeds, cuttings, and other plant parts.
Kikuyu: Ūyū nī mūtaratara wa gūciarithia na kūingīhia mīmera kuuma ihumo-inī ta mbeū, irūngo, na icunjī ingī cia mīmera.

English: Mangoes can be propagated in two ways: sexually, that is, through seeds, and asexually.
Kikuyu: Mīembe īciarithanagio kūhetūkīra njīra igīrī; kūhūthīra mbeū na gūciarithania gūtarīkūhūthīra mbeū.

English: In mango farming, propagation is primarily done by seeds especially for the indigenous varieties.
Kikuyu: ūrīmi-inī wa maembe, mīembe īciarithanagio kuuma mbeū-inī, na makīria kuuma mīembe-inī ya mīthemba ya kīndūire.

English: Exotic varieties are obtained by successfully grafting a scion on the indigenous rootstock.
Kikuyu: Mīembe ya mūciarithanio yumaga kūrī kūgwatithania mīrūri na itina cia kīndūire.

English: This leads to development of the various dwarf trees.
Kikuyu: ūndū ūyū ūgatūma mītī mīingī īkorūo īrī mīkuhī.

English: During planting, spacing may vary from 5m by 5m to 8m by 8m depending on the growth habit of the variety.
Kikuyu: Mīembe no ītaganio na mita ithano kwa ithano nginya mita inyanya kwa inyanya kūringana na mūthemba.

English: When are grafted seedlings ready for transplanting?
Kikuyu: Hihi mīūngūrwa ya mīembe mīciarithanie īhandagwo rī?

English: - Grafted seedlings are ready for transplanting out to the field after 4 – 6 months
Kikuyu: Mīūngūrwa ya mīembe mīciarithanie no ihandwo kuuma mweri wa kana nginya wa gatandatū.

English: - A mango tree must never be transplanted while it is flushing or when the leaves are still tender
Kikuyu: Mūngūrwa ndūbatiī gūthamio ūtwarwo kūhandwo mūgūnda-inī ūtarutīte mathangū.

English: - Transplant after the second flush has hardened
Kikuyu: Thamia mīūngūrwa thutha wa kūruta mathangū ma keerī.

English: - Transplant at the beginning of rains or water the holes before planting to ensure contact of the roots with moist soil
Kikuyu: Handa mīūngūrwa mbura yaringa, kana wambe wūite maaī irima-inī nīguo mīri īkore tīīri mūigū.

English: - Plant the young tree into the hole and fill the hole and press the soil around the stem firmly
Kikuyu: Handa mīūngūrwa irima-inī, ūrīihūrie na tīīri na ūkindīre hanini.

English: - Irrigation is necessary in absence of adequate rainfall for proper establishment of the young trees.
Kikuyu: Mīmera īno īitīīrīrio maaī angīkorwo gūtirī mbura kana angīkorwo mīri ndīnyitīte wega.

English: Mulching around the tree is recommended to smother weeds and to conserve moisture
Kikuyu: Kwarīra mahuti ma mūcing'i nīkwagīrīire nīguo kūgiria ria kūmera na kūiga maaī tīīri-inī.

English: - Trees should be top dressed with 250g of CAN per tree at the beginning of each rainy season after attaining a height of 1 metre. However, soil analysis is recommended.
Kikuyu: Mītī īno mīnini ībatiī kūheo bataraitha mūthemba wa CAN mūigana wa gramu magana merī ma mīrongo ītano o mbura īkīringa thutha wa mītī gūkinyia ūraihu wa mita īmwe. īndī tīīri nīwagīrīire gūthimwo.

English: Propagation materials for mangoes?
Kikuyu: Nīindo irīkū ibatiīhīndīya gūciarithania mīembe?

English: Certified mango seedlings are acquired from registered nurseries.
Kikuyu: Mīūngūrwa mīciarithanie yonekaga kuuma kūrī natharī iria hetūkie.

English: The farmer can top-work the already existing mango trees to introduce recommended varieties.
Kikuyu: Mūrīmi no aciarithanie mītīyake ya kīndūire na mīthemba īngīirīa yendekithītio.

English: Farmers can also plant seeds (local varieties) at farm and later graft using scions from registered mother blocks.
Kikuyu: mūrīmi no ahande mbeū cia kīndūire acooke aciciarithanie na mīrūri kuuma kūrī mīgūnda īrīa mīhītūkie.

English: What is grafting in mangoes ?
Kikuyu: Gūciarithania kwa mīembe nīgwīka atīa?

English: Grafting is a vegetative propagation technique to multiply trees that have identical characteristics with the desired parent tree.
Kikuyu: īno nī njīra ya gūciarithania mīembe ūkīhūthīra īcunjī kuuma kūrī mīthemba ya mītī īngīīrīa īrīna maūthī marendeka.

English: It involves placing a portion of one plant (bud or scion) on a stem or branch of another (stock) in such a way that a permanent union is formed and the partners will continue to grow
Kikuyu: Mūrūri kuuma kūrī mūtī ūrīa ūrendeka wīkīragwo gītina-inī kīa mūtī wa kīndūire na njīra īkūhotithia mītī īno yerī kūgwatana.

English: Benefits of  grafting in mangoes ?
Kikuyu: Mawega ma gūciarithania kwa mūthemba ūyū nī marīkū?

English: • Useful in plant propagation
Kikuyu: Nī njīra ya bata ya kūingīhia mītī.

English: • Trees develop more resistance to pests and soil diseases
Kikuyu: Mītī nīīhotaga gwītiria tūtambi na mīrimū ya kuuma tīīri-inī.

English: • Improves the genetic and physiological properties of trees
Kikuyu: Nīgūtūmaga mītī īkorūo na hinya nīūndū wa kūgarūrīra ūrīa īrutaga wīra.

English: • Increases productivity
Kikuyu: Mītī nīyongagīrīra maciaro.

English: • Allows homogeneity in fruit production
Kikuyu: Kūhotithia mūbango wa kūhanda na gūtungata mīembe ituīke īmwe.

English: What is top working and its benefits?
Kikuyu: Hihi top working nīkuuga atīa na kūrehaga ūguni ūrīkū?

English: Top working is the practice of grafting the top portion of a mature mango tree or one large enough to have several branches.
Kikuyu: īno nī njīra ya gūciarithania mūembe mūkūrū mwena wa naigūrū, kana ūrīa mūnene ūrī na honge ciigana ūna.

English: The procedure involves decapitating a mature tree so that new branches emerge.
Kikuyu: Gūkū nī gūtinia mūtī mūkūrū nīguo honge njerū ciume.

English: The technique is used to convert an already established long-lived mango tree into another variety or cultivar.
Kikuyu: Nī mūtaratara ūhūthīkaga kūgarūra mūembe mūkūrū ūtuīke wa mūthemba ūngī.

English: The benefits include: One tree may produce different varieties of fruit
Kikuyu: Mawega ma ūndū ūyū nī hamwe na atī mūtī ūmwe no ūkūrīrio mīthemba ngūrani ya maembe.

English: • Takes a shorter period for the tree to mature
Kikuyu: Mūtī woyaga ihinda inini gūkūra.

English: • Helps maintain a healthy plant with a strong root system
Kikuyu: Mūtī nīūkoragwo na hinya nīūndū wa gītina kīrī na mīri mīega.

English: • Helps rejuvenate old mango trees
Kikuyu: Nīkwerūhagia mītī mīkūrū.

English: What materials are needed for mango grafting?
Kikuyu: Nī indo īrīkū ībatarainie hīndī ya gūciarithania mīembe?

English: Usually, mango seedlings are grafted using top/ wedge grafting method.
Kikuyu: Kaingī mīūngūrwa ya mīembe īciarithanagio na njīra ya kūmīrenga mītwe.

English: Required tools and materials: Pruning scissors, scalpel or grafting knife.
Kikuyu: Indo iria ībataranagia nī hamwe na magathī ma gūceha na kahiū.

English: Grafting tape or soft clear polythene bag cut in strips.
Kikuyu: Tepu ya kūgwatia kana tūratathi twa mūnyore tūta rīna rangi na tūceke.

English: Which month is best for mango grafting?
Kikuyu: Mīembe yagīrīirwo gūciarithanio mweri-inī ūrīkū?

English: Propagation of mango by modified cleft grafting during 15 June showed higher percentage of survival in compare with cleft and veneer grafting irrespective of varieties.
Kikuyu: Gūciarithania mūembe na Modified Cleft Grafting mweri 15 wa gatandatū nīwonanirie kūgwatania wega gūkīra Cleft na Veneer gūtakūmakania nī mūthemba ūrīkū wa mūtī.

English: The percentage of success was, however, higher in June by modified cleft grafting but in August by veneer grafting
Kikuyu: O na kūrī ūguo, gīcunjī kīa ūgacīru nīkīarī kīnene mūno mweri-inī wa Juni na njīra ya modified cleft grafting no mwerī-inī wa īnana, gūciarithania na veneer gūkīgacīra makīria.

English: Which grafting technique is best for mango?
Kikuyu: Hihi nī njīra īrīkū njega ya gūciarithania mīembe?

English: A wide range of graft types can be used on mangoes, but the two most common are the whip and the cleft or wedge graft.
Kikuyu: Nīkūrī njīra nyingī cia gūciarithania mīembe, no njīra īrīa īhūthagīrūo kaingī nī īrīa mūrūri wīkīragwo thīinī wa cleft kana wedge.

English: The whip graft is used widely by nursery operators and other highly experienced operators, while the wedge graft is used by most other grafters.
Kikuyu: Kūhūthīra whip gūciarithania mīembe kūhūthagīrwo mūno nī arīa marutaga wīra natharī-inī cia mītī na angī arīa marī na ūmenyeru mūingī, no arīa angī maciarithanagia makīhūthīra njīra ya wedge.

English: Both grafts are quite easy to do.
Kikuyu: Njīra cīerī cia gūciarithania nī hūthū.

English: When is the main production period of mangoes in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Kīmera kīrīa kīnene kia maembe Kenya kīambagīrīria rī?

English: There are two supply seasons.
Kikuyu: Gūkoragwo īmera īgīrī:

English: The first and main season runs from November to February and the second from June to August.
Kikuyu: Kia mbere kīambagīrīria mwerī wa ikūmi na ūmwe nginya wa mweri wa kerī nakīo gīa keerī ngīkambīrīria mweri wa juni nginya wa īnana.

English: In areas of higher altitude such as Murang'a and Mwea(Central Province), the harvest season is 4—6 weeks later than at the coast areas, with a peak in February and March.
Kikuyu: Matūra marīa marī igūrū ta Murang’a na Mwea (gītaro gia gatagatī gia būrūri wa Kenya) maembe magethagwo wiki inya nginya ithathatū thutha wa matūra ma gīcua-inī kūrīa maembe maingīhaga mweri-inī wa kerī nginya wa ītatū.

English: The mango picking season in Kenya competes with that of other mango producing countries (Mexico, Brazil, India, Pakistan, Israel, South Africa) and extends over a period of between 5 and 6 months
Kikuyu: Kīmera kia maembe kia būrūri wa Kenya kīrīa kīngīikara mīeri ītano nginya ītandandū, nī gīcindanaga na īmera cia mabūrūrī mangī ta Mexico, Brazil, India, Pakistan, Israel, South Africa.

English: Why do I have a lower production of mangoes?
Kikuyu: Nīkīī kīratūma magetha kuuma mīembe-inī yakwa manyihe?

English: Productivity depends on a number of factors, including quantity of previous crop, weather and soil conditions, altitude, control of pests and diseases, fertilization and cultivar.
Kikuyu: Kūnyiha kwa magetha kūrehagwo nī maundū maingī hamwe na mūigana wa magetha ma kīmera kīhītūku, ūrīa rīera na tīīrī ihana, matūra kūrīa maembe marakūrīrio, mīrimū na tūtambi, ūhei wa bataraitha na mūthemba wa mīembe.

English: Even in the case of the same cultivar, yields vary greatly because mango is grown under widely varying agro climatic conditions and cultural practices.
Kikuyu: Ona angīkorwo mīembe nīya mūthemba ūmwe ,mūigana wa magetha no ūkorwo na ūtiganu nīūndū mīembe īkūragio matūra-inī marī na rīera na mītungatīre ītiganīte.

English: Why do mangoes production differ in a year?
Kikuyu: Nīkīī gītūmaga magetha ma maembe makorūo marī na ngūrani o mwaka?

English: Biennial or irregular bearing occurs often with the mango and it is common for some cultivars to bear heavily in one year and sparsely the next.
Kikuyu: Kaingī mīembe īciaraga maita merī o mwaka kana īgaciara ītekūrūmīrīra mūtugo ūyū na nīngī nī harī mīthemba īmwe ya mīembe iciaraga mūno mwaka ūmwe īgacoka īkanyihia maciaro mwaka ūrīa ūmīrīire.

English: One of the reasons for this phenomenon is that trees over-bear in one year, thus inhibiting adequate flower bud formation the following year.
Kikuyu: Gītūmi kīmwe nīatī mīembe īngīcira īkīrie nī īremagwo kūruta kīro mwaka ūrīa ūrūmīrīire.

English: Under these circumstances, it is difficult to get accurate local long-term yield records.
Kikuyu: Nīūndū wa maūndū macio, ti ūndū mūhūthū kūmenya ūhoro wīgiī magetha ma ihinda iraya.

English: However, it is well known that yields of 25 t/ha and more for Kent, Sabine, Tommy Atkins and Keitt are not uncommon.
Kikuyu: Ona kūrī ūguo, hactare īmwe no īgethwo tani mīrongo īrī na ītano ona makīria ya maembe mūthemba wa Kent, Sabine, Tommy Atkins na Keītt.

English: How long do mangoes take to mature after flowering?
Kikuyu: Mīembe yoyaga ihinda rīigana atīa gūkūra thutha wa kūruta kīro?

English: Depending on cultivars and environmental conditions it takes 90 to 160 days after flowering for Kenya mangos to reach maturity.
Kikuyu: Kūringana na mūthemba na marīa marigicīirie mūgūnda, mīembe no yoe thikū 90 – 160 gūkūra kuuma rīrīa kīro kīaumire.

English: Not all fruits on one tree will ripen at the same time.
Kikuyu: Maembe marī mūti-inī ūmwe matiīruhanagīra.

English: A great problem is to determine precisely the stage at which the fruit is ripe for picking.
Kikuyu: Thīīna ūrīa mūnene nī kūmenya ihinda rīrīa iembe rīagīrīire gūtuo rīarīkia gūkūra.

English: When is the best time to pick or harvest mango fruits?
Kikuyu: Maembe mabatiī gūtuo kana kūgethwo rī?

English: The fruits are generally picked when they begin to change colour.
Kikuyu: Kaingī maembe matuagwo magarūrūka rangi?

English: This may occur first in a small area or the change will cover most of the fruit's surface.
Kikuyu: Iembe no rīambīrīrie kūgarūrūka rangi handū hanini rangi ūcio ūcoke ūtūngane itunda rīothe.

English: What is the yield of mangoes planted in one īka?
Kikuyu: īka īmwe ya mīembe igethagwo maembe maigana atīa?

English: An īka mango trees yield is from 12,000 kgs to 18,000kg (500 to 800 fruits per tree per year) in a year for a 5-year orchard
Kikuyu: īka īmwe ya mīembe īgethagwo maembe gatagatī-inī ga kiro 12,000-18,000 (maembe 500 – 800 o mūembe o mwaka) kuuma kūrī mītī ya ūkūrū wa mīaka ītano.

English: What is desapping or delatexing
Kikuyu: Hihi desapping kana delatexing nī gwīka atīa?

English: It is the removal of fresh latex from the fruit by either inverting freshly de-stemmed fruits on plastic or steel mesh racks, allowing the latex to drip for 30 minutes under shade.
Kikuyu: Nīkwanīka maembe kīruru-inī matuo na njīra ya kūmainamia igūrū wa iratathi rīa mūnyore kana rūtara-inī rwa steel kwa ihinda rīa dagīka mīrongo ītatū nīguo maniare maathina.

English: What is trimming during harvest of mangoes?
Kikuyu: Gūtema mīembe hīndīya magetha nīkuuga atīa?

English: At harvesting fruit is clipped from the tree to leave 3 to 4 cm stalk attached to the fruit which is later reduced (trimming) to 1cm during final packing
Kikuyu: Hīndī ya gūtuo, maembe matigithanagio na mūtī na njīra ya kūrenga kamūtī karīa kacinyitithanītie gagatigwo kari na ūraihu wa sentimita 3-4 iembe-inī karīa gacokaga kūnyihio gagatigwo karī na ūraihu wa sentimita īmwe.

English: How do you identify mature fruits?
Kikuyu: Maembe makūrū mamenyekaga atīa?

English: 1. Fruit shape- mature fruit have flat shoulders at the stem end and full cheeks (Ngowe).
Kikuyu: 1. Mūhianīre wa iembe– iembe ikūrū rīkoragwo rīrī na ciande njigananu mwena wa mūti-inī na makai maiyūru (Ngowe).

English: 2. Fruit Peel appearance-peel has white powdery substance, peel color change from dark green to pale green
Kikuyu: 2. Mūhianīre wa ikoro – ikoro rīgakorwo na mūtutu mwerū na rangi ūkagarūrūka kuuma grini nduru nginya ngirini hunyūku.

English: 3. Fruit pulp - pulp color change from white to light creamy yellow.
Kikuyu: 3. Ngogoyo ya iembe-Ngogoyo īcenjagia kuuma rangi mwerū nginya rangi wa kīrimū.

English: 4. Built shoulders- slight depression near the stalk in mature fruit (Apple).
Kikuyu: 4. Ciande njakīku wega– Maembe mūthemba wa Apple magīaga na kariko kanini mwena ūrīa menyiterīire kūrī mūti.

English: What is the maturity period of mangoes?
Kikuyu: Maembe makūraga na ihinda rīigana atīa?

English: It takes 95 to 115 day from flowering to fruits maturity
Kikuyu: Mayoga thikū 95-115 kuuma maruta kīro nginya iembe gūkūra.

English: When to harvest mangoes?
Kikuyu: Maembe magethagwo rī?

English: Harvesting starts 3-4 years after transplanting.
Kikuyu: Magethagwo kwambīrīria mwaka wa gatatū nginya wa kana kuuma marutwo natharī-inī.

English: Fruits are ready 3-5 months from flowering depending on the variety.
Kikuyu: Mīthemba ngūrani ya maembe īkūraga thutha wa mīeri ītatu nginya ītano kuuma rīrīa mūtī waruta kīro.

English: Fruit should be clipped from the tree leaving 3 to 4 cm stalk attached to the fruit which is later reduced to 1 cm
Kikuyu: Maembe matuagwo magītīgagīrio kamūti karī na ūraihu wa sentimita ithatū nginya inya karīa thutha-inī gakūhīhagio gagatigio karī ka sentimita īmwe.

English: Best way to harvest mangoes fruits?
Kikuyu: Hihi njīra njega ya kūgetha maembe nī īrīkū?

English: Use harvesting tools to help protect fruits at harvesting.
Kikuyu: Hūthīra indo iria ciagīrīire nīguo ūgitīre maembe ūkigetha.

English: Use a basket on a long pole with a cutting tool attached, any blades used for harvesting should be sharpened regularly to minimize damage to the fruit and tree.
Kikuyu: Hūthīra gīkabu kīohereirwo rūbito-inī rūraihu hamwe na kahiū ga gūtua karīa karīnoragwo kaingī nīguo itunda kana mūtī ndūgatihio.

English: Proper training before harvesting is key to using harvesting tool correctly.
Kikuyu: Nīūndū wa bata gūthomithia atui ūrīa mabatiī kūhūthīra indo cia gūtua.

English: Harvest fruits early in the day to keep them cool and reduce water loss.
Kikuyu: Maembe mendaga kugethwo rūci-inī tene nīguo maikare marī mahehu na matigate maaī.

English: Why does mango turn black after cutting?
Kikuyu: Maembe mairaga nīkī matuo?

English: Trapped inside the tissue of fruits are molecules known as enzymes.
Kikuyu: Thīinī wa matunda, nīkūrī tūindo tūītagwo enzymes.

English: These enzymes help fruit ripen and turn brown.
Kikuyu: Enzymes nīcio itumaga matunda meruhe na magaruruke rangi matuīke ma gītīri.

English: When fruit is cut or starts to break down, the enzyme is released from the tissue of the fruit and exposed to air which causes the fruit to rapidly change color.
Kikuyu: Itunda rīngī temwo kana rīngīambīrīria kūbutha, enzymes irekagīrīrio kuuma thīinī wa itunda igatukana na rīera, igatūma itunda rīgarūrūke rangi naihenya.

English: This is known as enzymatic browning
Kikuyu: Ūndū ūyū wītagwo enzymatic browning.

English: How do you control fruit cracking in mangoes?
Kikuyu: Gwatūka kwa maembe kūngīrigīrīrio atīa?

English: Pre-harvest sprays of naa, potassium and boron and maintaining adequate soil moisture during the dry period were found to be beneficial in reducing cracking in fruit crops.
Kikuyu: Kūhūra dawa ta naa, potassium na boron mbere ya kūgetha na kūreka tīīri ūige wūigū rīrīa kūrī na riūa ihiū nīkūonekanīte kūrehe ūguni mūnene harī kūgiria maembe gwatūka.

English: What is the best suitable nursery for growing mangoes?
Kikuyu: Natharī īrīa njega ya kūhurīra maembe īhana atīa?

English: To select a suitable nursery site, many factors must be considered the most important being mother block, size, water, soil, slope and access.
Kikuyu: Gūthura handū hega ha gūthondeka natharī, no mūhaka ūrore maūndū maingī mamwe marīa ma bata meta ūnene wa mūgūnda, gūkūhīrīria maaī, tīīri, o na kūharūrūka kwa mūgūnda.

English: This determines the success or failure of a plant nursery:
Kikuyu: Nīkī kīngīrehe kūgaacīra kana kwaga kūgaacīra kwa natharī?

English: - A nursery should be isolated as far as possible from existing commercial planting and should be accessible
Kikuyu: Natharī ībatiī kūraihanīrīria na mīgūnda īrīa īkūragio maembe ma biacara na ningī īkorwo handū harakīnyīrīka.

English: - Movement of vehicles and personnel through the nursery should be limited
Kikuyu: Gūcera kwa ngari na andū thīinī wa mūgūnda wa natharī gūbatiī kūnyihanyihio.

English: - No foreign materials should be allowed into the nursery without proper precautions
Kikuyu: Indo ng'eni itigetīkīrio kūingīra mūgūnda-inī wa natharī itathuthurītio wega.

English: - Tools and equipment should frequently be sterilized
Kikuyu: Indo ciothe cia kūruta wīra ciagīrīirūo gūtherio wega maita maingī.

English: - The nursery should be built on a gentle slope to avoid flooding
Kikuyu: Natharī yagīrīirwo gūthondekwo handū hainamu hanini nīguo ndīkaihūre maaī ma mbura.

English: - Water should be available and free of harmful pathogens, nematodes and fungi
Kikuyu: Maaī makorwo hakuhī na matigakorwo na tūtambi kana njamuthi.

English: How to selection and manage mother plants of mangoes
Kikuyu: ūrīa ūngīthuura na gūtungata mīmera īrīa īhūthagīrūo gūciarithania maembe?

English: Prior to seed collection, one needs to select and mark good mango mother trees to use as sources of high quality seed and scions.
Kikuyu: Mbere ya gūthura mbeū, mūndū nīarabatara gūthura na gūīkīra rūūri mītī īrīa mīega īrīhūthagīrwo kūruta mbeū na mīrūri ya gūciarithania.

English: The major characteristics of a good mother tree are:
Kikuyu: Ngumo iria nene cia mūtī mwega wa gūciarithania nī hamwe na:

English: - Mature
Kikuyu: -Gūkūra kwa mūtī

English: -  Free of pests and diseases
Kikuyu: -Ndūrī na tūtambi na mīrimū

English: - Vigorous
Kikuyu: -Gūkūra na hinya

English: - True-to-type
Kikuyu: -Nī mūthemba ūrīa wagīrīire

English: - High yielding
Kikuyu: -Gūciara mūno

English: How to manage and control mango weevil?
Kikuyu: Njīra ya kūhiūrania na mboca cia iembe nī īrīkū?

English: Females lay eggs on young fruit which hatch to the larva that enters the fruit by burrowing through the flesh into the seeds, where it feeds until pupation, destroying the seed, leading to premature fruit fall.
Kikuyu: Mboca ya nga īrekagīria matumbī maembe-inī marīa macarīkio matuīkaga tūgunyū tūrīa tūingīraga itunda-inī na kūrīrīa nginya rīkagwa rītarī rīrakūra

English: Attack remains undetected in the field, and is seen when cutting the fruit.
Kikuyu: Ūndū ūyū wūikaraga ūtamenyekete ūrimi-inī nginya ihinda rīa kūgetha itunda rīkinye

English:
Cultural control
-Collect fallen fruits and destroy by burying or burning
Kikuyu: Gūthaitīra mūrimu ūyū na njīra cia kīīmerera.ūngania matunda maya na ūmathike kana ūmacine

English: -Paint the tree trunk with agriculture lime at flowering to prevent the pest from climbing
Kikuyu: Haka mīmera yaku ndimū hīndīya  kīro nīgetha kūrigīriria tūtambi tūtū gūtamba mūmera

English: -Applying sticky bands at the upper end of tree trunks when the trees start flowering to reduce migration of weevils to branches for egg laying.
Kikuyu: Haka ngamu mīthia-inī ya gītina hīndī īria mūmera waambīrīria kūruta kīro nīgetha kūrigīriria tūtambi tūtū gūtamba ona kana gūthama kuuma rūhonge-inī rūmwe nginya rūria rūngi

English:
Chemical control
-Spray insecticides before flowering and fruit set and repeat every 14 days interval use; Abamectin, Deltamethrin, Lambda cyhalothrin, Acetamiprid Emamectin
Kikuyu: Gūthaitīra na dawa.Huhīra mīmera yaku ītarutīte kīro na ūcokere thutha wa thikū ikūmi na inya ūkīhūthiraga dawa ta Abamectin, Deltamethrin Lambda cyhalothrin,Acetamiprid Emamectin

English: How to manage and control mango Gall fly?
Kikuyu: Ingīrūa atīa na ngi mūthemba wa gall fly ya maembe?

English: The pest causes the leaf to produce wart-like galls resulting to reduced photosynthesis.
Kikuyu: Tūtambi tūtū tūhumbīraga mathangū na galls ūndū urīa ūgiragīa ūthondeki wa īrio guthiī na mbere wega.

English: Severe infestation can result to total defoliation.
Kikuyu: Tūtambi tūngīgumīra mūmera wīwothe nīkuuga mūtī ūcio ndūkanahota gwīthondekera irio.

English: Adult fly lays eggs on the young leaf tissues.
Kikuyu: Ngi ng'ima irekagia matumbī mathangū-inī marīa matarī makūrū.

English: Once the eggs hatches the larvae burrow in the leaf and gall formation begins in 7 days.
Kikuyu: Matumbī macarīkaga igunyū iria cingīraga mathangu-inī na thutha wa thikū 7 gall ikambīrīria gwīthondeka.

English: Control: Spray Lexus® 247SC 8 ml/20l + Integra® 3 ml.
Kikuyu: Uthūthīri wa dawa. Huhīra mīmera na dawa ya Lexus®247SC 8ml itukanīte na lita 20 cia maaī na wongerere Integra® 3ml

English: Note: Proper timing is very crucial and this should be done at the onset of flushing.
Kikuyu: Ririkana kūhuhira ihinda rīrīa rīagīrīire mīembe īkīambīrīria kūruta kīro nīūndū nīguo ūndū ūria ūrehaga maumīrīra mega.

English: How to manage and control Mealybugs?
Kikuyu: Hihi ingīrūa na tūtambī mūthemba wa mealybugs atīa?

English: They are tiny soft bodied insects which tend to mass together in to a cottony looking mass on mango plants.
Kikuyu: Tūtū nī tūtambi tūkoragwo twītūhoro na tūikaraga handū hamwe na tūkahana ta bamba mūtī-ini wa mūembe

English: They are sucking pests and their feeding activities cause slow plant growth and can kill twigs and new leaves.
Kikuyu: Tūtambi tūtū tūnyuaga maaī ma mūmera ūndū ūrīa ūtūmaga mūti gūkūra kahora

English: Heavy infestation lead to sooty mould on leaves, fruits and twigs as a result of honeydew produced.
Kikuyu: Tūtambi tūtū tūngikorwo tūrī tūingī tūhumbagīra mathangū, matunda na tūhonge ūndū ūria ūtūmaga hakorwo na wūigū (honey dew)

English: Control: Spray Emerald® 200SL 10 ml/20l or Lexus® 247SC 8 ml/20L plus Integra® 3ml/20L.
Kikuyu: Kūhūthīra dawa: Huhīra dawa ya Emerald® 200SL 10ml itukanītion lita-inī 20 cia maaī kana Lexus ® 247AC 8ml lita-inī 20 cia maaī.

English: How to manage and control Mango scales?
Kikuyu: Hihi ingīrūa na ngūci atīa?

English: They are limpet-like insects with shell-like waxy covering which conceals their body.
Kikuyu: Tūtū nī tūtambi tūkoragwo tūhana ta twīna kīndū gītūhumbīire

English: They feed by sucking the plant sap hence robbing plants their essential nutrients.
Kikuyu: Tūtambi tūtū tūnyuaga juice irīa īkoragwo īkūīte irio cīa mūmera

English: They weaken or even kill the plant especially at the early stages.
Kikuyu: ūndū ūyū nīwagithagia mūmera hinya kana ona kūūraga makīria rīrīa ūrī mūnini.

English: Control: Spray Emerald® 200SL 10 mls/20L or Loyalty® 700 WDG 5 g/20L with Integra® 3 ml.
Kikuyu: Kūhūthīra dawa:Huhīra dawa ya Emerald ® 200 SL 10mls ītukanītio lita-inī 20 cia maaī kana Loyalty ®700 WDG 5g harī Lita 20 na interga ® 3ml

English: How to manage and control Thrips in mangoes?
Kikuyu: Ingīrūa na tūtambi mūthemba wa thrips mīembe-inī atīa?

English: Thrips are slender, minute insects with fringed wings and unique asymmetrical mouth parts.
Kikuyu: Thrips nī tūtambi tūkoragwo na mathagu marī na mīthia īna guaya na kanua karī na mūthece

English: They cause damage to plants by puncturing and sucking their contents.
Kikuyu: Tūthūkagia mūmera na njīra ya gūtūra na kūnyua maaī ma mūtī

English: They lead to flower abortion and scaring rusty appearance on fruits.
Kikuyu: Tūtūmaga mūmera wūite kīro narīo ikoro rīkahunyūka.

English: Control: Spray Alonze® 50EC 5 ml/20L or Lexus® 247SC 10 ml/20L plus Integra® 3ml/20L
Kikuyu: Kūgitīra: Huhīra dawa ya Alonze®50EC 5ml ītukanītio na lita 20 cia maaī kana Lexus ®700WDG 5 na ūtukanie na Integra ® 2ml harī lita 20 cia maaī.

English: How to manage and control Red spider mites?
Kikuyu: Ingīrūa na tūtambi mūthemba wa mbūmbūī ndūne atīa?

English: They are members of acari family.
Kikuyu: Tūtū nī tūtambi twa mbarī ya mbūmbūī.

English: They generally live on the underside of the leave.
Kikuyu: Tūikaraga mwena wa nathī wa ithangū

English: May spin protective silk web.
Kikuyu: No tūthondeke rūrenda rwa kwīgitīra.

English: They cause damage by puncturing plant cells and scarring the young fruit.
Kikuyu: Tūthūkagia mūmera na njīra ya gūtūra na gūīkīra ngūraro matunda marīa manini.

English: Control: Spray Alonze® 50EC 5 ml/20 + Integra® 3 ml/20l.
Kikuyu: Ūhūthīri wa dawa: Huhīra dawa mīthemba ya Alonze ® 50EC ūtūkanītie mililita ithano lita-inī 20 cia maaī na wongerere Integra ® mlilita 3.

English: How to manage and control Mango fruit fly?
Kikuyu: Ingīrūa na kīhuruta gīa maemba atīa?

English: Female fruit flies puncture the skin of mature ripe fruit and lay eggs that hatch into maggots (larvae) that feed on flesh destroying the fruit.
Kikuyu: Kīhuruta kīa nga gītūraga ikoro rīa itunda rīrīa ikūrū na kūrekia matumbī thīinī marīa macokaga magacarīka igunyū iria īrīaga itunda na kūrībuthia.

English: The affected fruit fall prematurely.
Kikuyu: Itunda rīrī rīcokaga rīkagwa rītarī ikūrū.

English: The female fly lays eggs under the skin of mango fruit.
Kikuyu: Tūtambi tūtū turekagia matumbī ngothi-inī ya itunda

English: Eggs hatch into whitish maggots which feeds on the fruit.
Kikuyu: Matumbī maya macarīkaga igunyū njerū iria irīaga matunda.

English: This leads to rotting of the fruits.
Kikuyu: Ūndū ūyū ūtūmaga itunda rībuthe.

English: Fruit flies are currently the most important pests of mangoes.
Kikuyu: Ihinda-inī rīrī, ihuruta nītuo tūtambi twamwanya ūrīmi-inī wa mūembe tūrīa tūbatiī kūrūo na tūo.

English: Control: An integrated approach should be employed to manage the pest, keeping the orchard clean and use of pheromone traps are some of the methods.
Kikuyu: Kūgitīra: Ūrabatara kūrūmīrīra mītaratara nīgetha wone maumīrīra mega, wambere tigīrīra atī mūgūnda waku nī mūtheru na wūige mitego ya tūtambi.

English: Chemical control: Spray Pentagon® 50 EC 10 ml/20l, Lexus® 247SC 8 ml/20l or Occasion Star® 200SC 5 ml/20l plus Integra® 3ml.
Kikuyu: Ūhūthīri wa dawa.Hūhira Pentagon®50 EC mililita 10 ūtukanītie na lita 20 cia maaī,Lexus®247 SC mililita 8 lita-inī 20 cia maaī, kana Occassion star®200SC milita 5 lita-inī 20 cia maaī na ūtukanie na Integra ® 3ml

English: How to manage and control powdery mildew?
Kikuyu: Kūrūa na mūrimū wa powdery mildew.

English: Affects leaves, and flowers of mango plants.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū ūgūmagīra mathangū na kīro kīa mīembe.

English: Usually starts off as white spots.
Kikuyu: Waambagīrīria na karoro kanini kerū.

English: Affected plants look as if they have been dusted with white substance.
Kikuyu: Mītī īrīa īgūmīirwo īhanaga ta īitīīrīirio mūhu mwerū

English: Infected plant show patches of white powdery fungal growth on leaves, flowers and young fruits, old leaves and fruit display purple-brown cast.
Kikuyu: Mītī īrīa īgūmīirwo yonanagia tūroro twerū mathangū-inī, matunda-inī na mahua-inī namo mathangū marīa makūrū mokoragwo na rangi wa purple na gītīri.

English: -This fungus appears mostly in warm, humid weather.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū wūingīhaga rīrīa ūrugarī na ūgūnyu ciaingīha rīera-inī.

English: In severe attacks, the entire blossom panicle may be involved and fruit fail to set (affecting yields).
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū ungītherema mūno nīūnyihagia maciaro ma mūtī.

English: They lead to defoliation of young leaves and severe flower abortion if not controlled.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū ūtumaga mathangū marīa manini magaitīka hamwe na mahūa ūngīaga kūgirio gūtherema.

English: Control: Spray Absolute® 375SC 10 ml/20L, Ransom® 600WP 10 g/20L, or Ducasse® 250EW 20 ml/20L with Integra® 3ml/20L.
Kikuyu: Ūhūthīri wa dawa.Huhīra Absolute ®375 SC mililita 10 ūtukanītie na lita 20 cia maaī, Ransom® 600WP gramu 10 hari lita 20 kana Ducasse® 250 EW mililita 20 harī lita 20 cia maaī na ūtukanie na Integra ® mililita 3 harī o lita 20 cia maaī.

English: How to manage and control Anthracnose
Kikuyu: Ingīhūrana atīa na mūrimū wa Anthracnose?

English: It’s a fungal infection which may affect the stem, leaves, flowers or even the fruit.
Kikuyu: ūyū nī mūrimū wa fungal na ūgūmagīra honge, mathangū, mahūa kana matunda.

English: It is characterized by dark sunken lesions on ripe fruits, sunken chlorotic lesions on leaves or drying up of flowers.
Kikuyu: Ūmenyekaga nīūndū wa ironda horokeru matunda-inī marīa meru, mathangū-inī na mahūa kūūma.

English: Control: Ransom® 600WP 10 g/20l or Absolute® 375SC 10 ml/20l + Integra® 3ml
Kikuyu: Ūhūthīri wa dawa.Huhīra Ransom ® 600WP gramu 10 harī lita 20 cia maaī kana Absolute ® 375SC mililita 10 lita-inī 20 cia maaī na ūtukanie na Integra ® mililita 3.

English: How to manage and control Die Back in mango?
Kikuyu: Ingīhūrana atīa na mūrimū wa mīembe kūūma īrutītie mīthia-inī?

English: This disease is characterized by drying up of twigs from top to bottom of particular older Plants followed by drying of the leaves which give an appearance of fire scorch.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū ūmenyekaga na njīra ya honge kūūma īrutītie mīthia-inī makīria ya mītī īrīa mīkūrū na kūrūmīrīrwo nī mathangū marīa mahanaga tarī mahīu nī mwaki.

English: If left uncontrolled, dieback can lead to death of the whole tree.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū ūngīaga kūhūranwo nagwo, nīūtūmaga mūtī ūūme ūrī wothe.

English: Control: Prune the affected twigs 3 inches below infection site and spray Green Cop® 500WP 50 g/20L or Trinity Gold® 452WP 50 g/20L.
Kikuyu: Ūgitīīri.Ceha thari ciothe īrī na ūraihu wa inji 3 na ūhuhīre dawa mūthemba wa cop®500WP ūtukanītie gramu 50 lita-nī 20 cia maaī kana Trinity Gold® 425WP gramu 50 thīinī wa maaī lita 20.

English: Which is the leading county producer of mangoes in Kenya
Kikuyu: Nī county īrīkū itongoretie ūkūria-inī wa maembe kenya?

English: The leading mango producing counties are: Makueni, Lamu, Kilifi, Meru, Kitui, Tana River, Kwale, Machakos, Elgeyo Marakwet, Siaya, Migori, Baringo, Bungoma, Embu, Tharaka Nithi, Kirinyaga, Muranga, Taita, Taveta, Garissa counties
Kikuyu: County irīa itongoretie ūrīmi wa maembe nī Makueni, Lamu, Kilifi, Meru, Kitui, Tana River, Kwale, Machakos, Elgeyo Marakwet, Siaya, Migori, Baringo, Bungoma, Embu, Tharaka Nithi, Kirinyaga, Murang'a, Taita Taveta, na Garissa

English: What is the recommended spacing for Mango Farming
Kikuyu: Ubatiī kūhanda mīembe na wariī wa mūigana ūrīkū kuuma harī mūembe ūmwe nginya harī ūrīa ūngī?

English: The spacing varies from 10 X 10 M to 13 X 13 M. In dry areas, the spacing should be 10 X 10 M due to less growth.
Kikuyu: Wariī wa mita 10 gwa 10  nginya mita 13 gwa 13. Matūra-inī marīa matonaga mbura nyingī, ūtaganu wa mita 10 x 10 nīgūo wagīrīire nīundū wa mītī kwaga kūneneha.

English: But, in heavy rainfall area & fertile soil, the spacing should be 13 X 13 M due to higher physical growth.
Kikuyu: No kūndū kūrī mbura nyingī na tīīri mūnoru, kūbatiī wariī wa mita 13 gwa 13 nīundū wa kūneneha mūno.

English: Do mango spacing depends on the variety?
Kikuyu: Hihi ūtaganu wa kūhanda wa mīembe nīūringanaga na mūthemba wa mūembe?

English: Depending on varieties and the area climatic conditions, spacing of 8 -12m × 8-12m is recommended giving a population of 36 – 70 trees per īka.
Kikuyu: Kūringana na mūthemba wa mīembe na rīera rīa itūra, ūtaganu wa mita 8-12 kwa mita 8-12 nīguo wagīrīire itina 36 nginya 70 o īka.

English: How to plant a mango tree?
Kikuyu: Ubatiī kūhanda mīembe atīa?

English: The ideal period to plant a mango tree is during the months of March, April, and May when the climate is moderate.
Kikuyu: Ihinda rīega rīa kūhanda mīembe nī mweri wa gatatū, wa kana na wa gatano rīrīa gūtarī mbura nene kana riūa rīingī.

English: Nevertheless, it’s crucial to ensure that the mango tree is not susceptible to any frost during the planting process.
Kikuyu: Ningī ndukariganīrwo nīwagīrīire gūtigīrīra atī mītī ndīragūmīrwo nī mīrimū ya heho hīndī ya kūhanda.

English: Selecting a Planting Site: Mango trees prefer a sunny spot with loose, well-draining soil.
Kikuyu: Gūthura handū ha kūhanda mīembe: Handū haha habatiī gūkinyīrwo nī riūa na hakorwo na tīīri ūraikūrūkia maaī wega.

English: Consider the tree’s mature size when selecting a planting site, and note the site’s proximity to other plants and structures.
Kikuyu: Nīwega kūmenya mūembe ūkaigana atīa warīkia gūkūra, ona ningī kūmenya ūrīa ūgakorwo ūraihanīrīirie na mīmera īrīa īngī na mīako.

English: Container growth is an option for the smaller mango tree varieties Spacing, Depth, and Support:
Kikuyu: Kūhanda mīkebe-inī nīkwega na mīembe īria ītaraihaga muno, ītarī mīri mīriku na ītabataraga gūtirīrīrwo.

English: Spacing depends on the mango variety you’re growing.
Kikuyu: ūtaganu ūringanaga na mūthemba wa mūembe

English: Check the mature canopy width, along with the height, to make sure you’ll have enough room to grow your tree.
Kikuyu: Rora ūraihu wa mūmera ūrīa ūrahanda nīgetha ūmenye mūigana wa ūtaganu ūrīa ūkūhanda naguo

English: Saplings should be planted in their nursery container at the same depth they were growing.
Kikuyu: Mbegū cīa kūmerithia ibatiī kūhandwo na ūriku ūiganaine na harīa ūrakinyīte.

English: It would be best if you planted seeds about 1/2 inch deep.
Kikuyu: Nīkwagīrīire ūhande mūngūrwa waku na ūriku wa nuthu iji.

English: Saplings might need staking for support as they grow, especially in an area with strong winds.
Kikuyu: Kūhanda mūngūrwa rīmwe nīkūbataraga kuhandīrwo tūmītī twa kwīnyitīīrīra mūno kūndu kwīna rūhuho rūungī

English: How many mango trees can be planted in one īka?
Kikuyu: Ubatiī kūhanda mīembe īigana harī īka īmwe

English: Depending on varieties and the area climatic conditions, spacing of 8 -12m × 8-12m is recommended giving a population of 36 – 70 trees per īka.
Kikuyu: Kūringana na mūthemba na rīera, ūtaganu wa mita 8-12 gwa 8-12 nīguo ūbatiī kūhanda mītī 36 nginya 70 īka īmwe.

English: What are the economic Importance of Mangoes?
Kikuyu: Maembe makoragwo na ūguni ūrīko kībiacara?

English:
Consumed as fresh fruits
Source of income
Kikuyu: Mahūthagīrwo guthondeka juice.

English: Source of foreign exchange
Kikuyu: Kūrehe mbeca cia mabūrūri ma naja.

English: Source of employment
Kikuyu: Nī matūmaga andū magīe mawīra.

English: Combats nutritional disorders
Kikuyu: Nī irio njega ona hīndī ya ndwari

English: Mango to health benefits are;
Kikuyu: Nī marī ūguni mūnene mwīrī-inī.

English: High in antioxidants that protect your cells against free radical damage.
Kikuyu: Nī marī indo cia bata iria itūmaga mwīrī ūhote gwītiria mogarūrūku ma hi na hi.

English: Scientific research confirms that radical damage leads to signs of aging and chronic diseases such as cancer.
Kikuyu: ūthuthuria nī wonanītie atī mogarūrūku ma hi na hi nī matūmaga mūndū oneke arī mūkūrū makīrīa ona kūrehe mīrimū ta cancer

English: Mango is a great source of immune-boosting nutrients.
Kikuyu: Maembe nī marī indo itūhotithagia mwīrī kūrūa na mīrimū.

English: It is a good source of several B vitamins, as well as vitamins A, C, E, and K- all of which help boost your immunity.
Kikuyu: Maembe ni marī vitamin B,A,C,E na K ciothe nīirīmandini magiragia mwīrīkūgūmirwo nīmīrimu

English: Mangiferin, an antioxidant found in mangoes, may protect heart cells against inflammation, apoptosis (controlled cell death), and oxidative stress.
Kikuyu: Mangiferin, īrīa yumaga kūrīembe nī īteithagīrīria mīrimū ya ngoro,na kūgirīrīria gūkua gwa cells na stress irīa irehagwo nī wagi wa rīera thīinī wa cells.

English: It also contains a group of digestive enzymes called amylase.
Kikuyu: Ningī nīīrī na gīkundi kīa enzyme cia gūthea irio ciītagwo amylase.

English: Moreover, water and dietary fiber in the fruit aid digestive health.
Kikuyu: Makīria ma ūguo, maaī na baimba īrīa ī thīinī wa matunda nīīteithagia mwīrī gūthīa irio.

English: The enzymes break down large food molecules for easy absorption.
Kikuyu: Enzymes iteithagia kūnyihia tūcunjī tūrīa tūnene twa irio nīguo mwīrī ūhote gūcihūthīra wega.

English: Why sort mango fruits?
Kikuyu: Nīkīī kīngītūma tūthuranie maembe?

English: Fruits are sorted to remove diseased, misshaped, immature fruits, bruised fruits and any foreign material
Kikuyu: Matunda mathuranagio na njīra ya kweheria marīa marwaru, marīa matarī na mūhianīre mwega, marīa matarī makūrū, marīa menanguraro, na kīndū o gīothe kīngī gītabatiī gūkorwo thīinī wamo.

English: How do you grade mango fruits?
Kikuyu: Maembe macagūranagio na irathi atīa?

English: Grade mangoes based on the standard criteria accepted by the industry.
Kikuyu: Maembe macagūranagio na irathi kūringana na ithimi iria ciītīkīrītwo cia thoko.

English: Three standard mass grades can be set as: large (over 300 g), medium (150–300 g) and small (below 150 g).
Kikuyu: Irathi ithatū no ithondekwo hamwe na: Manene (maritū gūkīra gramu 300), Ūritū wa gatagatī(gramu 150-300) na manini (ūritū ūnyihīire gram 150).

English: Minimal defects such as wind scar and latex stain may be accepted.
Kikuyu: Mawathe manini nīmetīkīrikaga ta ironda nīūndū wa rūhuho na mīcoora ya methina

English: Fruits are graded according to size, colour and texture (class I and II).
Kikuyu: Maembe maigagwo na irathi cia ūnene, rangi na ūrīa mahana(kīrathi kīa I na II)

English: What is the best way to transport mango fruits?
Kikuyu: Njīra njega ya gūkua maembe nī īrīkū?

English: Use covered vehicles to avoid direct sunlight heat which cause damage to the fruits.
Kikuyu: Hūthīra ngari irīa ngunīke nīguo ūhote gwīthema ūrugarī wa riūa ūrīa ūngīthūkia maembe.

English: Minimize fruits contact with soil to avoid contamination that causes decay and safety concerns.
Kikuyu: Tigīrīra atī maembe matirahutania na tīīri nīguo matigabuthe kana kūrehe ūgwati mīrīre-inī.

English: Use clean plastic crates for fruit transportation. Refrigeration is encouraged
Kikuyu: Hūthīra mathandūkū matheru ma plastīki rīrīa ūrakua matunda. Kūngīhoteka, ūhehia ngari īrīa īramakua.

English: How do you store mango fruits?
Kikuyu: Maembe maigagwo atīa?

English: Use clean crates during storage. Mango can be stored under refrigeration at 12˚C.
Kikuyu: Hūthīra mathandūkū matheru rīrīa ūramaiga. Maembe no maigwo thīinī wa friji īrī na ūrugarī wa gīthimo kia digirii 12 ̊C.

English: Separate ripe from unripe mangoes and other ripe fruits to slow down ripening by reducing exposure to ethylene.
Kikuyu: Amūrania maembe marīa meru na marīa matarī meru na matunda mangī marīa marīkītie kwīīruha nīguo matikeruhanie naihenya nīūndū wa ethylene.

English: What are the benefits of treating mango fruits using hot water?
Kikuyu: Nī ūguni ūrīkū ūrehagwo nī ūhūthīri wa maaī mahiū kūhonia maembe mīrimū?

English: Mango hot water treatment involve immersing mango fruit in water bath at temperature of 46.1 0C for 68, 75 and 84 min depending on variety.
Kikuyu: Maembe maikagio thīinī wa maaī mahiū marī na ūrugarī wa digirii 46.1oC kwa dagīka 68, 75 na 84 kūringana na mūthemba.

English: Hot water treatment is used to minimize fruit fly damage by killing the egg and also for control of anthracnose and stem end rot on harvested fruits.
Kikuyu: Ūhūthīri wa maaī mahiū ūteithagia kūnyihia tūtambi mūthemba wa fruit fly na njīra ya kūūraga matumbī ma tuo na ningī kūhonia mīrimū ya anthracnose na kūbutha mīkori ya maembe matuo.

English: What are the features (yields, average weight and flavor) of Ngowe mango variety?
Kikuyu: Nī ngumo irīkū (cia maciaro, ūritū na mūcamo) ikoragwo na maembe mūthemba wa Ngowe?

English: Ngowe or the Lamu mango is the common mango cultivar along the Kenyan coast.
Kikuyu: Ngowe īrīa yūīkaine ta iembe rīa Lamu, nī īmwe ya mīthemba ya maembe īrīa īkūragio mwena wa gīcua-inī gīa Kenya.

English: They prefer sandy, well-drained soils and can survive with little rainfall.
Kikuyu: Mūthemba ūyū wendete tīīri ūtukanīte na mūthanga na ūraikūrūkia maaī wega na ningī no nīwīkaga wega ona gūtarī mbura nyingī.

English: Its trees are small sized with a well rounded canopy.
Kikuyu: Mītī ya Ngowe nī mīnini na mahuti mathondekaga gīthiūrūrī kīega.

English: The variety is a low land variety and does well in low altitude areas.
Kikuyu: Ngowe nī mūthemba wīkaga wega kūndū gūtarī irīma.

English: The tree has medium yields and alternate bearing may occur.
Kikuyu: Mītī ya ngowe ndīkoragwo na magetha maingī na rīngī īgaciara wega kīmera-inī kīmwe kūrī kīrīa kīngī.

English: The fruits are orange to yellow-coloured when ripe.
Kikuyu: Matunda ma ngowe magarūrūkaga rangi meruha magakorwo ma orange na ngoikoni.

English: They are oblong and slender in shape with a prominent beak.
Kikuyu: Maembe ma ngowe nī maraihu na maceke, na mīthia mīcomoku.

English: The Ngowe mango flesh is deep yellow, free from fibre and of excellent quality for use as fresh market and for processing.
Kikuyu: Itunda rīa ngowe rīkoragwo na rangi mūthaka wa ngoikoni, rītirī ndigi na nīrīega mono wendia-inī wa thoko cia matunda na kūhūthīka gūthondeka indo ingī.

English: The variety is moderately tolerant to anthracnose but susceptible to powdery mildew
Kikuyu: Mūthemba ūyū nīūhotaga gwītiria hanini mūrimū wa anthracnose no ndūhotaga gwītiria mūrimū wa powdery mildew.

English: Altitude; 0-800 metres above sea level Harvest Season; November to March Fruit Weight; 425—600 g (mean: 523 g) Fruit Measures; 14 cm long and 9.5 cm wide
Kikuyu: Altitude; mita 0-800 igūrū wa iria, Ihinda rīa Kūgetha; Mweri wa ikūmi na īmwe nginya wa ītatū, ūritū wa maembe; gramu 425-600 (ūritū wa gatagatī: gramu 523) Mūigana wa iembe; ūraihu nī sentimita 14 na wariī nī sentimita 9.5.

English: What are the features (yields, average weight and flavor) of Tommy Atkins mango variety?
Kikuyu: Nī ngumo irīkū (cia maciaro, ūritū na mūcamo) ikoragwo na maembe mūthemba wa Tommy Atkins?

English: Tommy Atkins is an early to mid-season cultivar that is high yielding (400-600 fruits/tree/season).
Kikuyu: Tommy Atkins nī mūthemba ūciaraga matunda maingī mūno, na ūciaraga matunda gatagatī-inī kana kīambīrīria-inī gīa kīmera, na kaingī ūciaraga matunda 400 nginya 600 o mūtī o mwaka.

English: The tree is large and has a compact rounded canopy.
Kikuyu: Mūtīwa Tommy Atkins nī mūnene na nīūtumanagia mahuti na ūthondekaga gīthiūrūrī kīega.

English: The variety is slightly tolerant to anthracnose disease and is best suited for cultivation in mid altitude areas (800-1600 metres above sea level).
Kikuyu: Mūthemba ūyū nīūnyitagwo nī mūrimū wa anthracnose na wagīrīire kūhandwo matūra marīa marī mita 800-1600 igūrū rīa iria.

English: It bears fruits consistently which matures between Jan-March.
Kikuyu: Ūciaraga matunda ūrūmanīrīirīe marīa makūraga gītagatī-inī kīa Janūari na mweri wa ītatū.

English: The fruits are orange/yellow with a heavy red blush and numerous white lenticels.
Kikuyu: Maembe ma Tommy Atkins makoragwo marī ma rangi wa orange kana ngoikoni na mīkurura ya rangi mūtune na tūthundo tūingī twerū.

English: Tommy atkins fruits are sweet and fibreless, medium to large sized with an average weight of 522g, oval to oblong shaped (average length 12.6 cm and width 9.9 cm) and a broadly rounded base.
Kikuyu: Maembe ma Tommy Atkins makoragwo na camaa na nī marī ndigi, makoragwo marī na ūnene wa gatagatī-inī na ūritū ta wa gramu 522, mūhianīre wa itumbī kana oblong(na ūraihu wa sentimita 12.6 na warīe wa sentimita 9.9) na makoragwo marī na gītina kīariī na gīthiūrūrī.

English: Altitude; 0-800 metres above sea level Harvest Season; November to March Fruit Weight; 425—600 g (mean: 523 g) Fruit Measures; 14 cm long and 9.5 cm wide Yields; 400-600 fruits/tree/season
Kikuyu: Altitude; mita 0-800 igūrū wa iria, Ihinda rīa Kūgetha; Mweri wa ikūmi na ūmwe nginya wa gatatū, ūritū wa maembe; gramu 425-600 (ūritū wa gatagatī: gramu 523) Mūigana wa iembe; ūraihu nī sentimita 14 na wariīnīsentimita 9.5. Mūtī ūciaraga gatagatī ka maembe 400-600 o kīmera.

English: What are the features (yields, average weight and flavor) of Vandyke mango variety?
Kikuyu: Nī ngumo irīkū (cia maciaro, ūritū na mūcamo) ikoragwo na maembe mūthemba wa Vandyke?

English: Vandyke is a large tree with an open canopy that is best suited for cultivation in mid altitude areas (800-1600 metres above sea level).
Kikuyu: Vandyke nī mūtī mūnene ūtakoragwo na mahuti matumanu, na ūkūraga wega matūra-inī marīa marī mita 800-1600 igūrū wa iria.

English: It is a mid-season variety that is moderate yielding.
Kikuyu: Nī mūthemba ūkūraga gītagatī-inī gīa kīmera na ndūciaraga mūno.

English: The variety is a regular bearer and comes into bearing in January-March.
Kikuyu: Mūthemba ūyū ūciaraga kaingī kwambīrīria Janūari nginya mweri wa gatatū.

English: The Van Dyke fruits are small to medium sized, with an oval shape and a prominent beak.
Kikuyu: Maembe ma Vandyke nī manini, mūhianīre wa itumbī na mūthia mūcomoku.

English: When ripe, they are bright yellow with a heavy crimson blush, covered by numerous yellow/white lenticels.
Kikuyu: Marīkia gūkūra, magarūrūkaga rangi makakorwo na ngoikoni īrahenia na mīkurura mītune mīcamu īkūnīkītwo nī tūthundo tūingī twa rangi wa ngoikoni kana twerū.

English: The fruits are sweet with little fibre and strong a pleasant aroma.
Kikuyu: Maembe ma vandyke makoragwo na mūcamo mwega, matirī ndigi nyingi na nīmarī mūtararīko mūnene mwega.

English: Altitude; 800-1600 meters above sea level Harvest Season; January to March Fruit Weight; 280 g Fruit Measures; 10.5 cm long and 7.6 cm wide Yields; 28-400 fruits/tree/season
Kikuyu: Altitude; mita 800-1600 igūrū wa iria, Ihinda rīa Kūgetha; Januari nginya mweri wa ītatū, ūritū wa maembe; gramu 280 Mūigana wa iembe; ūraihu nī sentimita 10.5 na wariī nī sentimita 7.6. Mūtī ūciaraga gatagatī ka maembe 28-400 o kīmera.

English: What are the features (yields, average weight and flavor) of the Kent mango variety?
Kikuyu: Nī ngumo irīkū (cia magetha, ūritū na mūcamo) ikoragwo na maembe mūthemba wa kent?

English: The Kent is a high yielding, late season mango variety that comes into bearing in late season.
Kikuyu: Mīembe ya Kent īciaraga mūno, nīguo mūthembe ūrīa ūciaraga kīmera gīgīthira.

English: The tree has a tendency of alternate bearing.
Kikuyu: Mūtī ūyū nīūrī mūtugo wa kwaga gūciara kīmera-inī kīmwe no ūgaciara kīu kīngī.

English: The tree is large and has an upright canopy.
Kikuyu: Mūembe wa Kent nī mūnene na honge ciagwo ikoragwo irūngiī.

English: The variety is best for growing in mid altitude areas between 800-1600 ASL.
Kikuyu: Mūthemba ūyū ūkūraga wega matūra-inī marīa marī mita 800 nginya 1600 igūrū wa iria.

English: The Kent produces fruits that are large in size.
Kikuyu: Mūembe wa Kent ūciaraga matunda manene.

English: They are oval in shape.
Kikuyu: Matunda maya makoragwo na mūhiano wa itumbī.

English: They are greenish-yellow with a red/crimson blush and numerous yellow lenticels.
Kikuyu: Nī ma rangi wa ngirini na ngoikoni na mīkurura ya rangi mūtune na makanyakanya ma rangi wa ngoikoni.

English: Some advantages of growing the fruits is because they are sweet, fibreless, strong flavored and have a long shelf life.
Kikuyu: Mawega ma gūkūria mīembe ya Kent nīatī nī marī cama, matirī ndigi, mūtararīko mūnene na matithūkaga naihenya

English: Altitude; 800-1600meters above sea level
Kikuyu: Makūraga wega matūra-inī marīa marī mita 800 nginya 1600 igūrū wa irīa.

English: Harvest Season;  late February-April
Kikuyu: Ihinda rīa kūgetha; mūisho wa mweri wa kerī nginya wa kana.

English: Fruit Weight; 545g
Kikuyu: Ūritū wa iembe; gramu 545.

English: Fruit Measures; 12.4 cm long and 9.7 cm wide
Kikuyu: Ithimo cia iembe; ūraihu nī sentimita 12.4 na ūtungu nī sentimita 9.7.

English: Yields; 450-800 fruits/tree/season
Kikuyu: Maciaro; mūtī ūmwe nīūciaraga maembe 450-800 o mwaka o kīmera.

English: What are the features (yields, average weight and flavor) of Keitt mango variety?
Kikuyu: Nī ngumo irīkū (cia maciaro, ūritū na mūcamo) ikoragwo na maembe mūthemba wa Keitt?

English: Keitt mango variety is a moderately vigorous tree. It has long arching branches and a natural scraggy open canopy.
Kikuyu: Mūembe wa keitt ūkūraga wega. Ūkoragwo na honge ndaihu na mahuti matarī matumanu.

English: The Keitt variety is best suited for cultivation in mid altitude areas.
Kikuyu: Mūthemba wa keitt nī mwagīrīru kūhandwo matūra-inī matarī irīma-inī nene.

English: It is a high yielding and late season variety that bears fruits regularly.
Kikuyu: Nī mūtī ūciaraga mūno na ūciaraga kīmera gīgīthira na ningī ūciaraga matunda kaingī.

English: The fruits are large sized with an average weight of 456g, plump and oval shaped.
Kikuyu: Matunda nī matungu na ūritū wa gramu 456, na mūhano wa itumbī.

English: They are greenish-yellow with a red/pink blush and numerous white, yellow or red lenticels.
Kikuyu: Nī ma rangi wa ngirini na ngoikoni na mīkurura ya rangi mūtune kana pink na makanyakanya meerū, ngoikoni kana matune.

English: The flesh is deep yellow juicy and with a little fibre near the seed.
Kikuyu: Nyama ya maembe ma Keitt īkoragwo na rangi mūcamu wa ngoikoni na nī īrī ndigi nini hakuhī na gībegū.

English: Some benefits of the Keitt variety’s is its fair tolerance to anthracnose disease.
Kikuyu: Mawega mamwe ma mūembe mūthemba wa keitt nī gwītiria wega mūrimū wa anthracnose.

English: In addition, its fruits have a long shelf life.
Kikuyu: Ningī matunda magwo nīmaikaraga mūno mateguthūka.

English: On the downside, the Keitt fruits are susceptible to sunburn.
Kikuyu: Ūru wa matunda ma keitt nīatī nīmahotagwo na makangīa nguraro cia kūhīa nī riūa.

English: Altitude; 800-1600 metres above sea level
Kikuyu: Makūraga wega matūra-inī marīa marīmita 800 nginya 1600 igūrū wa irīa.

English: Harvest Season;  March-April
Kikuyu: Ihinda rīa kūgetha- mweri wa gatatū nginya wa kana.

English: Fruit Weight; 456 g
Kikuyu: Ūritū wa itunda; gramu 456

English: Fruit Measures; 11.7 cm long and 9.2 cm wide
Kikuyu: Itunda nīrīa ūraihu wa sentimita 11.7 na wariī wa sentimita 9.2.
