English: What are the features (yields, average weight and flavor) of the Apple mango variety?
Kikuyu: Nī ngumo irīkū (cia maciaro, ūritū na mūcamo) ikoragwo na maembe mūthemba wa Apple?

English: The Apple also known as Maharaja mango variety is a medium yielding and an early season variety.
Kikuyu: Apple, mūthemba ūrīa ūīkaīne ta maharaja, ndūciaraga mūno na ūkūraga tene kīambīrīria-inī gīa kīmera.

English: It shows a tendency to bear fruits alternately.
Kikuyu: Ūkoragwo na mūtugo wa gūciara matunda ūgītīragīria imera imwe.

English: The tree is large with a pyriform canopy.
Kikuyu: Mūembe wa apple nīūnenehaga na ūkarama mwene wa naigūrū.

English: The variety is is best suited for cultivation in low altitude areas
Kikuyu: Mūthemba ūyū ūkūraga wega matūra-inī marīa matarī kūndū gūtarī irīma.

English: The fruits are medium to large in size with an average weight of 397g and round in shape.

English:
•Well drained, deep soils
• pH range 5.5 – 7.5

tīīrī ūrahetūkia maaī wega
 ph ya kuuma 5.5 - 7.5

English: • Good drainage
Kikuyu: • Tīīrī ūrahītūkia maaī

Kikuyu: Batiī kūharīria natharī ya mīembe atīa?

English: Prepare a seedbed of width 1 m and a desirable length by removing farm soil from the seedbed to a depth of 30 cm and refill the 30 cm depression with sand
Kikuyu: Thondeka natharī ya wariī wa mita īmwe na ūraihu ūrīa ūkwenda na njīra ya kweheria tīīrī nginya ūriku wa sentimita mīrongo ītatū ūcoke wīihūrie irima rīu na mūthanga.

English: How do i prepare mango seed for planting?
Kikuyu: Batiī kūharīria mbegū cia mīembe atīa mbere cia kūhanda?

English: Pick fruit from the tree rather than from off the ground
Kikuyu: Thura maembe ma gūkūhe mbegū kuuma mūtī igūrū kūrī kūngania marīa magwīte thīī.

English: • Mango seed should be healthy, fresh and not dried at the time of planting
Kikuyu: • Mbegū ya maembe ībatiī gūkorwo ītari na mīrimū, matarī maikaru kuuma matuo na matarī momū hīndī ya kūhanda.

English: • Seeds must be taken from ripe fruits of mature, healthy and vigorous trees
Kikuyu: • Mbegū no nginya ciume kuuma kūrī maembe meru ma mītī mīkūrū, ītarī na mīrimū na īrīa īrakūra wega.

English: What are the cultivars used as root stocks in mango grafting?
Kikuyu: Itina iria ihūthagīrwo gūciarithania mīembe nī irīkū?

English: Cultivars used as rootstocks are: Peach, Sabre, Sikio punda and Dodo
Kikuyu: Itina njega ciumaga kūrī: peach, sabre, sikio, Punda na Dodo

English: What are the features (yields, average weight and flavor) of Sabine mango variety?
Kikuyu: Nī ngumo irīkū (cia maciaro, ūritū na mūcamo) ikoragwo na maembe mūthemba wa Sabine?

English: Sabine is a late maturing variety that bears fruits regularly.
Kikuyu: Sabine nī mūthemba ūkūraga ūrī mūcerere na kaingī ūciaraga ūtagūtīīrīria.

English: The tree has a vigorous growth with dense canopy.
Kikuyu: Mūtī wa sabine ūkūraga naihenya na ūkoragwo ūrī mūtumanu.

English: The Sabine Variety has some resistance to powdery mildew and anthracnose.
Kikuyu: Mūthemba wa Sabine nīūhotaga gūītiria mīrimū ya powdery mildew na anthracnose.

English: The Sabine variety is best suited for cultivation in mid altitudes.
Kikuyu: Mūthemba wa Sambine ūkūraga wega matūra-inī matarī īrīma-inī nene.

English: It produces fruits that are medium to large size.
Kikuyu: Ūciaraga maembe matarī manene mūno ona gūtwīka no manenehe.

English: They elongated in shape measuring 14.2cm and 6.6 cm.
Kikuyu: Makoragwo marī magucūku na ithimo cia sentimita 14.2 na 6.6.

English: The ripe fruits have a blight yellow and a red blush.
Kikuyu: Ikoro rīa maembe meeru rīkoragwo na rangi ūrakenga wa ngoikoni na mīkurura mītune.

English: The flesh is yellow, juicy, fibreless and with a mild flavour.
Kikuyu: Nyama ya itunda nī ya rangi wa ngoikoni, nīīrī cama, ndīrī ndigi na mūtararīko ūtarī mūnene.

English: What are the features (yields, average weight and flavor) of Haden mango variety?
Kikuyu: Nī ngumo irīkū (cia maciaro, ūritū na mūcamo) ikoragwo na maembe mūthemba wa Haden?

English: Haden is a high yielding and early season mango variety.
Kikuyu: Haden nī mūthemba ūciaraga mūno na ūkūraga tene kīmera kīambīrīria.

English: The tree grows large and has a spreading canopy.
Kikuyu: Mūtī nīūnenehaga na honge igakūra itambūrūkīte.

English: However, its susceptible to anthracnose.
Kikuyu: No ona kūrī ūgūo, nīūtoragio nī mūrimū wa anthracnose.

English: The variety is best suited for cultivation in mid altitude areas.
Kikuyu: Mūthemba ūyū ūkūraga wega matūra-inī matarī irīma-inī nene.

English: The fruits are medium to large sized and are ovate in shape.
Kikuyu: Maembe ma Handen nī manenehaga, na makoragwo na mūhano wa itumbī icomoku.

English: The fruit skins have a deep yellow with a deep crimson/red blush with whitish/yellow lenticels.
Kikuyu: Makoro ma Haden nī ma rangi wa ngoikoni īrahenia na mīkurura mītune mīcamu īkūnīkītwo nī makanyakanya merū kana ma rangi wa ngoikoni.

English: The flesh is deep yellowish orange, juicy and with an excellent aroma.
Kikuyu: Nyama ya iembe rīrī nī ya rangi wa orange, īī cama na mūnungo mwega mūno.

English: What are the features (yields, average weight and flavor) of Sabre mango variety?
Kikuyu: Nī ngumo irīkū (cia maciaro, ūritū na mūcamo) ikoragwo na maembe mūthemba wa Sabre?

English: Sabre is a high yielding and early harvesting mango variety.
Kikuyu: Sabre nī mūthembe ūciaraga mūno na ūkūraga tene kīmera kīambīrīria.

English: The tree is medium size and fairly resistant to diseases.
Kikuyu: Mūtī wa sabre ndūnenehaga mūno na nī wītiragia mīrīmū wega.

English: It is used as a rootstock to other mango varieties.
Kikuyu: Gītina gīa Sabre nīkīhūthagīrwo gūciarithania maembe mīthemba īngī.

English: The Sabre is an excellent rootstock variety that is used to create dwarfism in other varieties.
Kikuyu: Sabre nī mūthembe wī gītina kīega kīa ndūire kīrīa kīhūthīkaga gūkuhīhia mīthemba īngī ya mīembe.

English: The fruits are medium sized (average weight 180-290g), kidney-shaped with a prominent beak (average length 11.8cm and width 6.9cm).
Kikuyu: Maembe ma sabre nī ma ūnene wa gatagata-inī (gramu 180-290), memūhano wa higo na mūthia mūcomoku (ūraihu wa sentimita 11.8 na wariī wa sentimita 6.9).

English: They are yellow-green with a reddish blush.
Kikuyu: Makoro nī marangi wa ngoikoni na ngirini na mīkurura mītune.

English: The flesh is deep orange, juicy with moderate fibre and a turpentine after-taste
Kikuyu: Nyama ya iembe rīa sabre nī rīa rangi mūcamu wa orange, rī mūcamo mwega na ndigi nini na nīrītigaga mūcamo wa turpentine kanua.

English: What are the features (yields, average weight and flavor) of Peach mango variety?
Kikuyu: Nī ngumo irīkū (cia maciaro, ūritū na mūcamo) ikoragwo na maembe mūthemba wa Peach?

English: Peach also known as Zill Mango is a high yielding and early mango variety.
Kikuyu: Peach nī mūthemba wa iembe ūīkaine ta Zill ūrīa ūciaraga mūno na ūkūraga tene kīmera-inī.

English: The peach tree has a medium size and are fairly resistant to diseases.
Kikuyu: Mūembe wa Peach ndūnenehaga mūno na nī wītiragia mīrimū.

English: It is a common stock used as a rootstock to other mango varieties.
Kikuyu: Nī mūthemba ūhūthīkaga kaingī ta gītina gīa gūciarithania mīembe mīthemba īngī.

English: The fruits are small sized with an average weight of 80-100g.
Kikuyu: Maembe ma peach makoragwo manini na makinyagia ūritū wa gramu 80-100.

English: They are round-shaped with a depressed beak (average length 5.8cm and width 3.9cm).
Kikuyu: Memūhano wa mūbira na mūthia ūtarī mūcomoku (ūraihu wa sentimita 5.8 na wariī wa sentimita 3.9).

English: They are brown-green with a reddish blush.
Kikuyu: Ikoro nī rīa rangi wa gītīri kana ngirini na mīkurura mītune.

English: The flesh is deep orange, juicy with fibre and a sweet-taste
Kikuyu: Nyama ya īkoragwo na rangi mūcamu wa orange, mūcamo mwega na nīrīrī ndigi.

English: What are the features (yields, average weight and flavor) of Kitovu mango variety?
Kikuyu: Nī ngumo irīkū (cia maciaro, ūritū na mūcamo) ikoragwo na maembe mūthemba wa Kitovu?

English: Kitovu mango is a local mango variety commonly grown in Uganda and Tanzania.
Kikuyu: Kitovu nī mūthemba wa kīndūire ūrīa ūkūragio mūno mabūrūri-inī na Uganda na Tanzania.

English: Kitovu is a high yielding and early variety (December-April).
Kikuyu: Kitovu nī mūthemba ūciaraga mūno na ūkūraga tene kīmera-inī. (Dithemba nginya April)

English: The fruits are small sized (average weight 100-150g), kidney-shaped with a prominent end (average length 6.8 cm and width 4.9 cm).
Kikuyu: Maembe ma kitovu nī manini(ūritū wa gramu 100-150), mūhano wa higo na mūthia mūcomoku wega (ūraihu wa sentimita 6.8 na wariī wa sentimita 4.9).

English: They are yellow-green with a greenish blush.
Kikuyu: Makoragwo marī na rangi wa ngoikoni na ngirini na mīkurura ya ngirini.

English: The flesh is deep orange, juicy with fibre and a turpentine after-taste.
Kikuyu: Nyama ya kitovu īkoragwo īrī ya rangi ni ya icungwa rigwatu, mari cukari na ni mari cama muno

English: The tree is medium size and fairly resistant to diseases.
Kikuyu: Mūembe wa kitovu ndūnenehaga mūno na nī wītiragia mīrimū.

English: It is used as a rootstock to other mango varieties
Kikuyu: Nī mūthemba ūhūthīkaga ta gītina gīa gūciarithania mīembe mīthemba īngī.

English: What are the features (yields, average weight and flavor) of Kimji mango variety?
Kikuyu: Nī ngumo irīkū (cia maciaro, ūritū na mūcamo) ikoragwo na maembe mūthemba wa Kimji?

English: Turpentine also known as Manila mango variety is a high yielding variety that can withstand high salt levels compared to the rest.
Kikuyu: Mūthemba wa maembe wa Turpentine ūrīa woīkaine ta Manila ūciaraga mūno na nīūhotaga gwītiria tīri wīna cuumbī mūrūrū gūkīra mīthemba īngī ya maembe.

English: The fruit is small, round to ovate in shape and yellow in colour.
Kikuyu: itunda nī inini rīa gīthiūrūrī kana mūhano wa itumbī rīcomoku na nī rīa rangi wa ngoikoni

English: The yellow flesh is extremely fibrous, with a common classic flavour.
Kikuyu: Thīinī wa itunda riri rangi nī wa ichungwa na nīrīrī ndigi mūno na rīrī mūcamo ūmwe ūkūrīkanīte

English: Some turpentines carrying a stronger resin component while others sweeter, and with tolerable fibre.
Kikuyu: Turpentine imwe nī irī resin īna hinya na iri ingī nī irī cama mūno, na ndigi bacirīre

English: The tree exhibits dwarfing characteristics and fairly resistant to diseases. It is used as a rootstock to other mango varieties
Kikuyu: nī wītiragia mīrimū na nī ūhūthīkaga ta gītina harī gūciarithania mītī īngī

English: What mango varieties are dwarf?
Kikuyu: Nī mīthemba īrīkū ya maembe ikoragwo mīkuhī?

English: Dwarf varieties include Peach and Sabre, they have been developed by Kenya Agriculture and Livestock Research Organisation (KALRO).
Kikuyu: Mīthemba īno mīkuhī ya maembe nī hamwe na Peach na Sabre, īrīa īthondeketwo na īgathuthurio nī Kenya Agriculture and Livestock Research Organisation (KALRO).

English: The varieties are developed by grafting superior varieties onto local varieties.
Kikuyu: Mīthemba īno īthondeketwo na gūciarithania mītī mīega ya kwendeka na mītī ya tene.

English: Dwarf mango trees are ideal for small backyard gardens and their compact nature means that they will only reach 10 to 13 feet (three to four metres) tall.
Kikuyu: Mītī īno mīkuhī nī mīega mūno harī mīgūnda mīnini na nīūndū wa ūnyihu ūyū nī kuuga atī īgūkinya fiti 10 nginya 13 (MITA 3 nginya 4 harī ūraihu)

English: Their small size also means that you can easily cover them with netting to keep the birds away or grow them in a greenhouse in cooler climates.
Kikuyu: Ūnini ūyū wa matunda maya nī mwega tondū nī ūkūmahumbīra na neti nīguo kūgirīrīa nyoni kana gūkūrīria thīinī wa greenhouse kūrīa kūrī rīera rīega

English: Which varieties of Mangoes Grown in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī mīthemba īrīkū ya maembe ikūragio kenya?

English: Mango varieties grown in Kenya include Apple, Ngowe, Kent, Keitt, Tommy Artkins, Van Dyke, Haden, Sensation, Boribo, Sabine among others.
Kikuyu: Mīthemba ya maembe īrīa īkūragio Kenya nī ; Apple, Ngowe, Kent, Keitt, Tommy Artkins, Van Dyke, Haden, Sensation, Boribo, Sabine na mangī maaīngī

English: Main characteristics that differentiate varieties are the fruit shape, size, aroma, sweetness, colour, fibre content, taste, seed size and resistance to diseases.
Kikuyu: Maūndū ma kŪrora nīgūo gūtigithūkania mīthemba ya maembe nī mūhianīre wa itunda, ūraihu, mūnungo, cukari, rangi, mūigana wa ndigi, cama, ūnene wa mbegū na gūītiria mīrimū

English: Proper selection of a mango cultivar for production has to take into account the following criteria:
Kikuyu: Ūgīcagūra ihembe rīa kuhanda rīna maciaro mega no nginyagia ūrore maūndū maya:

English: good adaptation to the local conditions (e.g. rainfall and dry periods)
Kikuyu: Gūtwarana wega na rīera rīa kūu (mbūra na hīndī ya riūa)

English: alternation of flowering and fruiting
Kikuyu: Gūcenjania harī kūruta mahūa na kūruta matunda

English: tolerance to pest and disease infections
Kikuyu: Gwītiria tūtambi na mīrimū

English: designated use and market requirements
Kikuyu: Ūrīa itunda rīrenda kūhūthīrwo na ūrīa thoko īrenda

English: Mango varieties in Kenya
Kikuyu: Mīthemba ya maembe īrīa rīa īī Kenya

English: Kenyan mango varieties also referred to as mango cultivars, are divided into 3 categories.
Kikuyu: Mīthemba ya maembe gūkū Kenya nīūīkaine ta maembe ma kūhanda īgayanītio maita matatū

English:
There are:-
Early maturing – these mango cultivars mature from November to mid-January.
Kikuyu:
Na nīmo:-
Marīa maciaraga tene- maembe maya maciara kwambīrīria kīa November ngigya gītagatī kīa January.

English: In this category, we have Haden Mangoes, Ngowe Mangoes, Dodo Mangoes, Kensington Mangoes, Zill, and Apple Mangoes.
Kikuyu: Harī kīwango gīkī nīharī maembe ta Haden, Ngowe, Dodo, Kensington,Zill na ma Apple

English: Midseason – These mature between mid-January and late February.
Kikuyu: Maembe marīa maciaraga gatagatī-inī ga kīmera - maya makūraga gatagatī-inī ka mweri wa Janūari nginya mwisho-inī wa Feburuarī

English: Under this mango cultivar category, we have Sabine Mangoes, Peach Mangoes, Sabre Mangoes, Tommy Atkins Mangoes, Matthias Mangoes, Irwin Mangoes, Van Dyke Mangoes, Boribo Mangoes, and Smith Mangoes
Kikuyu: Harī mūthemba ūyū kūrī maembe ta Sabine Peach, Sabre, Tommy Atkins, Matthias, Irwin, Van Dyke, Boribo, na Smith.

English: Late maturing – There mature between Late February and April. They include Kent Mangoes, Sensation Mangoes, Zillate Mangoes and Keitt Mangoes.
Kikuyu: Maciaraga macereirwo kīmera-inī- maya makūraga mūicoinī wa kīmera, Janūari īgīthira nginya April. Maembe macio hamwe na Kent, Sensation, Zillate na Keitt
